text.NRSV: The proverbs of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
text.Rotherham: The proverbs of Solomon, son of David, King of Israel:
text.KJV: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;
text.NABRE: I. Title and Introduction Chapter 1 Purpose of the Proverbs of Solomon The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:
text.DRB: The Parables of Salomon, the son of David, king of Israel.

text.NRSV: For learning about wisdom and instruction, for understanding words of insight,
text.Rotherham: For the knowledge of wisdom and correction, for discerning the sayings of intelligence;
text.KJV: To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;
text.NABRE: That people may know wisdom and discipline, may understand intelligent sayings;
text.DRB: To know wisdom, and discipline:

text.NRSV: for gaining instruction in wise dealing, righteousness, justice, and equity;
text.Rotherham: For receiving the correction of prudence, righteousness and justice, and equity;
text.KJV: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;
text.NABRE: May receive instruction in wise conduct, in what is right, just and fair;
text.DRB: to understand the words of prudence: and to receive instruction of doctrine, justice, and judgment, and equity:

text.NRSV: to teach shrewdness to the simple, knowledge and prudence to the young—
text.Rotherham: For giving To the simple shrewdness, To the young man, knowledge and discretion.
text.KJV: To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.
text.NABRE: That resourcefulness may be imparted to the naive, knowledge and discretion to the young.
text.DRB: that subtility may be given to little ones, knowledge and understanding to the youngman.

text.NRSV: let the wise also hear and gain in learning, and the discerning acquire skill,
text.Rotherham: A wise man, will hear, and will increase learning, and a, discreet man, wise counsels, will acquire,
text.KJV: A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:
text.NABRE: The wise by hearing them will advance in learning, the intelligent will gain sound guidance,
text.DRB: the wise man hearing shall be wise: and he that understandeth shall possess governments.

text.NRSV: to understand a proverb and a figure, the words of the wise and their riddles.
text.Rotherham: By gaining discernment of proverb, and satire, the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.
text.KJV: To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.
text.NABRE: To comprehend proverb and byword, the words of the wise and their riddles.
text.DRB: He shall understand a parable, and interpretation, the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is the beginning of knowledge, Wisdom and correction, the foolish have despised.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
text.NABRE: Fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and discipline.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Fools despise wisdom and doctrine.

text.NRSV: Hear, my child, your father’s instruction, and do not reject your mother’s teaching;
text.Rotherham: Hear, my son, the correction of thy father, and do not reject the instruction of thy mother;
text.KJV: My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
text.NABRE: II. Instructions of Parents and of Woman Wisdom The Path of the Wicked: Greed and Violence Hear, my son, your father’s instruction, and reject not your mother’s teaching;
text.DRB: My son, hear the discipline of thy father, and leave not the law of thy mother:

text.NRSV: for they are a fair garland for your head, and pendants for your neck.
text.Rotherham: For, a wreath of beauty, shall they be to thy head, and chains of ornament, to thy neck.
text.KJV: For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.
text.NABRE: A graceful diadem will they be for your head; a pendant for your neck.
text.DRB: that grace may be added to thy head, and a chain of gold to thy neck.

text.NRSV: My child, if sinners entice you, do not consent.
text.Rotherham: My son, if sinners entice thee, do not consent.
text.KJV: My son, if sinners entice thee, consent thou not.
text.NABRE: My son, should sinners entice you,
text.DRB: My son, if sinners shall entice thee, condescend not to them.

text.NRSV: If they say, “Come with us, let us lie in wait for blood; let us wantonly ambush the innocent;
text.Rotherham: If they say—Come with us,—Let us lie in wait for blood, Let us watch in secret for him who is needlessly innocent;
text.KJV: If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:
text.NABRE: do not go if they say, “Come along with us! Let us lie in wait for blood, unprovoked, let us trap the innocent;
text.DRB: If they shall say: Come with us, let us lie in wait for blood, let us hide snares against the innocent without cause:

text.NRSV: like Sheol let us swallow them alive and whole, like those who go down to the Pit.
text.Rotherham: Let us engulf them, like hades, alive, While in health, like them who are going down to the pit;
text.KJV: Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:
text.NABRE: Let us swallow them alive, like Sheol, whole, like those who go down to the pit!
text.DRB: let us swallow him alive as hell, and whole as one descending into the lake.

text.NRSV: We shall find all kinds of costly things; we shall fill our houses with booty.
text.Rotherham: All costly substance, shall we find, We shall fill our houses with spoil;
text.KJV: We shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our houses with spoil:
text.NABRE: All kinds of precious wealth shall we gain, we shall fill our houses with booty;
text.DRB: We shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our house with spoils.

text.NRSV: Throw in your lot among us; we will all have one purse”—
text.Rotherham: Thy lot, shalt thou cast into our midst, One purse, shall there be, for us all.
text.KJV: Cast in thy lot among us; let us all have one purse:
text.NABRE: Cast in your lot with us, we shall all have one purse!”
text.DRB: Cast in thy lot with us, let there be one purse of us all.

text.NRSV: my child, do not walk in their way, keep your foot from their paths;
text.Rotherham: My son, do not walk in the way with them, Withhold thy foot from their path.
text.KJV: My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:
text.NABRE: My son, do not walk in the way with them, hold back your foot from their path!
text.DRB: My son, walk not with them, stay thy foot from their paths.

text.NRSV: for their feet run to evil, and they hurry to shed blood.
text.Rotherham: For, their feet, to mischief, do run, and haste to the shedding of blood.
text.KJV: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.
text.NABRE: [For their feet run to evil, they hasten to shed blood. ]
text.DRB: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.

text.NRSV: For in vain is the net baited while any bird looks on.
text.Rotherham: Surely, in vain, is spread the net, in the sight of aught that hath wings!
text.KJV: Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird.
text.NABRE: In vain a net is spread right under the eyes of any bird—
text.DRB: But a net is cast in vain before the eyes of them that have wings.

text.NRSV: But they lie in wait—for their own blood; they set an ambush—for their own lives.
text.Rotherham: Yet, they, for their own blood, lie in wait, They watch in secret for their own life.
text.KJV: And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives.
text.NABRE: They lie in wait for their own blood, they set a trap for their own lives.
text.DRB: Themselves also lie in wait against their own blood, & practise deceits against their own souls.

text.NRSV: Such is the end of all who are greedy for gain; it takes away the life of its possessors.
text.Rotherham: Such are the ways of every one that graspeth with greed, The life of the owners thereof, it taketh away!
text.KJV: So are the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; which taketh away the life of the owners thereof.
text.NABRE: This is the way of everyone greedy for loot: it takes away their lives.
text.DRB: So the paths of every covetous man, take violently the souls of the possessors.

text.NRSV: Wisdom cries out in the street; in the squares she raises her voice.
text.Rotherham: Wisdom, in the open place, soundeth forth, in the broadways, she raiseth her voice;
text.KJV: Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
text.NABRE: Wisdom in Person Gives a Warning Wisdom cries aloud in the street, in the open squares she raises her voice;
text.DRB: Wisdom preacheth abroad, she giveth her voice in the streets.

text.NRSV: At the busiest corner she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
text.Rotherham: At the head of bustling streets, she crieth aloud,—at the openings of the gates in the city—her sayings, she doth utter:—
text.KJV: She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying,
text.NABRE: Down the crowded ways she calls out, at the city gates she utters her words:
text.DRB: In the head of multitudes she cryeth, in the doors of the gates of the city she uttereth her words, saying:

text.NRSV: “How long, O simple ones, will you love being simple? How long will scoffers delight in their scoffing and fools hate knowledge?
text.Rotherham: How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and, scoffers, in scoffing, delight them? and, dullards, hate knowledge?
text.KJV: How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge?
text.NABRE: “How long, you naive ones, will you love naivete,
text.DRB: O children how long do you love infancy, and fools covet those things, which are hurtful to themselves, and the unwise hate knowledge?

text.NRSV: Give heed to my reproof; I will pour out my thoughts to you; I will make my words known to you.
text.Rotherham: Will ye turn, at my reproof? Lo! I will pour out to you my spirit, I will make known my words to you.
text.KJV: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you.
text.NABRE: How long will you turn away at my reproof? [The arrogant delight in their arrogance, and fools hate knowledge.] Lo! I will pour out to you my spirit, I will acquaint you with my words:
text.DRB: Turn ye at my correption: behold I will utter my spirit to you, and will shew you my words.

text.NRSV: Because I have called and you refused, have stretched out my hand and no one heeded,
text.Rotherham: Because I called, and ye refused, I stretched out my hand, and no one regarded;
text.KJV: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;
text.NABRE: ‘Because I called and you refused, extended my hand and no one took notice;
text.DRB: Because I called, and you refused: I stretched out my hand, and there was none that regarded.

text.NRSV: and because you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof,
text.Rotherham: But ye dismissed all my counsel, and, to my rebuke, would not yield,
text.KJV: But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:
text.NABRE: Because you disdained all my counsel, and my reproof you ignored—
text.DRB: You have despised all my counsel, and have neglected my reprehensions.

text.NRSV: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when panic strikes you,
text.Rotherham: I also, at your calamity, will laugh, I will mock, when cometh your dread;
text.KJV: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;
text.NABRE: I, in my turn, will laugh at your doom; will mock when terror overtakes you;
text.DRB: I also will laugh in your destruction, and will scorn, when that shall come to you, which you feared.

text.NRSV: when panic strikes you like a storm, and your calamity comes like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come upon you.
text.Rotherham: When cometh as a crashing your dread, and, your calamity, as a storm-wind, overtaketh, when there come upon you, distress and anguish.
text.KJV: When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.
text.NABRE: When terror comes upon you like a storm, and your doom approaches like a whirlwind; when distress and anguish befall you.’
text.DRB: When sudden calamity shall fall on you, and destruction as a tempest shall be at hand: when tribulation and distress shall come upon you:

text.NRSV: Then they will call upon me, but I will not answer; they will seek me diligently, but will not find me.
text.Rotherham: Then, will they call me, but I will not answer, they will seek me diligently, but shall not find me.
text.KJV: Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
text.NABRE: Then they will call me, but I will not answer; they will seek me, but will not find me,
text.DRB: Then shall they invocate me, and I will not hear: in the morning shall they arise, and shall not find me:

text.NRSV: Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Because they hated knowledge, and, the reverence of Yahweh, did not choose;
text.KJV: For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the Lord:
text.NABRE: Because they hated knowledge, and the fear of the Lord they did not choose.
text.DRB: for that they have hated discipline, and not received the fear of our Lord,

text.NRSV: would have none of my counsel, and despised all my reproof,
text.Rotherham: Consented not to my counsel, disdained all my rebuke,
text.KJV: They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.
text.NABRE: They ignored my counsel, they spurned all my reproof;
text.DRB: nor consented to my counsel, & detracted from all my correption.

text.NRSV: therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way and be sated with their own devices.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and, with their own counsels, be filled.
text.KJV: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices.
text.NABRE: Well, then, they shall eat the fruit of their own way, and with their own devices be glutted.
text.DRB: They shall eat therefore the fruits of their way, and shall be filled with their own counsels.

text.NRSV: For waywardness kills the simple, and the complacency of fools destroys them;
text.Rotherham: For, the turning away of the simple, will slay them,—and, the carelessness of dullards, will destroy them.
text.KJV: For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.
text.NABRE: For the straying of the naive kills them, the smugness of fools destroys them.
text.DRB: The aversion of little ones shall kill them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.

text.NRSV: but those who listen to me will live secure and will be at ease, without dread of disaster.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, he that hearkeneth unto me, shall dwell safely, and be at rest, without dread of misfortune.
text.KJV: But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil.
text.NABRE: But whoever obeys me dwells in security, in peace, without fear of harm.”
text.DRB: But he that shall hear me, shall rest without terror, and shall enjoy aboundance, fear of evils being taken away.

text.NRSV: My child, if you accept my words and treasure up my commandments with you,
text.Rotherham: My son, if thou wilt receive my sayings, and, my commandments, wilt treasure up by thee;
text.KJV: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 The Blessings of Wisdom My son, if you receive my words and treasure my commands,
text.DRB: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and wilt hide my commandments with thee,

text.NRSV: making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding;
text.Rotherham: So that thou direct, unto wisdom, thine ear, bend thy heart, unto understanding;
text.KJV: So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
text.NABRE: Turning your ear to wisdom, inclining your heart to understanding;
text.DRB: that thine ear may hear wisdom: incline thine heart to know prudence.

text.NRSV: if you indeed cry out for insight, and raise your voice for understanding;
text.Rotherham: Yea if, for understanding, thou cry aloud, for knowledge, utter thy voice;
text.KJV: Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, and liftest up thy voice for understanding;
text.NABRE: Yes, if you call for intelligence, and to understanding raise your voice;
text.DRB: For if thou shalt call for wisdom, and incline thine heart to prudence:

text.NRSV: if you seek it like silver, and search for it as for hidden treasures;
text.Rotherham: If thou seek her as silver, and, like hid treasure, thou search for her,
text.KJV: If thou seekest her as silver, and searchest for her as for hid treasures;
text.NABRE: If you seek her like silver, and like hidden treasures search her out,
text.DRB: If thou shalt seek her as money, and as treasures shalt dig her up:

text.NRSV: then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God.
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou understand the reverence of Yahweh, and, the knowledge of God, shalt thou find.
text.KJV: Then shalt thou understand the fear of the Lord, and find the knowledge of God.
text.NABRE: Then will you understand the fear of the Lord; the knowledge of God you will find;
text.DRB: then shalt thou understand the fear of our Lord, and shalt find the knowledge of God.

text.NRSV: For the Lord gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, giveth wisdom, out of his mouth, knowledge and understanding;
text.KJV: For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
text.NABRE: For the Lord gives wisdom, from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
text.DRB: Because our Lord giveth wisdom: and out of his mouth prudence and knowledge.

text.NRSV: he stores up sound wisdom for the upright; he is a shield to those who walk blamelessly,
text.Rotherham: Yea he treasureth, for the upright, safety, A shield [is he] to them who walk in integrity,
text.KJV: He layeth up sound wisdom for the righteous: he is a buckler to them that walk uprightly.
text.NABRE: He has success in store for the upright, is the shield of those who walk honestly,
text.DRB: He will keep the salvation of the righteous, & protect them that walk simply.

text.NRSV: guarding the paths of justice and preserving the way of his faithful ones.
text.Rotherham: To him that observeth the paths of justice, yea, the way of his men of lovingkindness, he doth guard.
text.KJV: He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.
text.NABRE: Guarding the paths of justice, protecting the way of his faithful ones,
text.DRB: Keeping the paths of justice, & guarding the ways of Saints.

text.NRSV: Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity, every good path;
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou understand righteousness and justice, and equity—every noble course.
text.KJV: Then shalt thou understand righteousness, and judgment, and equity; yea, every good path.
text.NABRE: Then you will understand what is right and just, what is fair, every good path;
text.DRB: Then shalt thou understand justice, and judgment, and equity, and every good path.

text.NRSV: for wisdom will come into your heart, and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
text.Rotherham: When wisdom entereth thy heart, and, knowledge, to thy soul, is sweet,
text.KJV: When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
text.NABRE: For wisdom will enter your heart, knowledge will be at home in your soul,
text.DRB: If wisdom shall enter into thy heart, and knowledge please thy soul:

text.NRSV: prudence will watch over you; and understanding will guard you.
text.Rotherham: Discretion, shall watch over thee, understanding, shall preserve thee:—
text.KJV: Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:
text.NABRE: Discretion will watch over you, understanding will guard you;
text.DRB: counsel shall keep thee, and prudence shall preserve thee,

text.NRSV: It will free you from the way of evil, from those who utter perverse things,
text.Rotherham: To rescue thee from the way of the wrongful, from the man that speaketh perverse things;
text.KJV: To deliver thee from the way of the evil man, from the man that speaketh froward things;
text.NABRE: Saving you from the way of the wicked, from those whose speech is perverse.
text.DRB: that thou mayst be delivered from the evil way, and from the man that speaketh perverse things:

text.NRSV: who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness,
text.Rotherham: From them who forsake the paths of rectitude, to walk in the ways of darkness;
text.KJV: Who leave the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness;
text.NABRE: From those who have left the straight paths to walk in the ways of darkness,
text.DRB: who leave the right way, and walk by dark ways:

text.NRSV: who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil;
text.Rotherham: Who rejoice to do wrong, exult in the perversities of the wrongful;
text.KJV: Who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the frowardness of the wicked;
text.NABRE: Who delight in doing evil and celebrate perversity;
text.DRB: who are glad when they have done evil, and rejoice in most wicked things:

text.NRSV: those whose paths are crooked, and who are devious in their ways.
text.Rotherham: Whose paths, are twisted, and they are tortuous in their tracks:
text.KJV: Whose ways are crooked, and they froward in their paths:
text.NABRE: Whose ways are crooked, whose paths are devious;
text.DRB: whose ways are perverse, and their steps infamous.

text.NRSV: You will be saved from the loose woman, from the adulteress with her smooth words,
text.Rotherham: To rescue thee, from the woman that is a stranger, from the female unknown, who with her speeches seduceth;
text.KJV: To deliver thee from the strange woman, even from the stranger which flattereth with her words;
text.NABRE: Saving you from a stranger, from a foreign woman with her smooth words,
text.DRB: That thou mayst be delivered from the strange woman, and from the foreigner, which mollifieth her words,

text.NRSV: who forsakes the partner of her youth and forgets her sacred covenant;
text.Rotherham: Who forsaketh the friend of her youth, and, the covenant of her God, hath forgotten;
text.KJV: Which forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the covenant of her God.
text.NABRE: One who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God;
text.DRB: forsaketh the guide of her youth,

text.NRSV: for her way leads down to death, and her paths to the shades;
text.Rotherham: For she hath appointed, unto death, her house, and unto the shades, her courses;
text.KJV: For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
text.NABRE: For her path sinks down to death, and her footsteps lead to the shades.
text.DRB: and hath forgotten the covenant of her God. For her house is bowed down to death, and her paths to hell.

text.NRSV: those who go to her never come back, nor do they regain the paths of life.
text.Rotherham: None who go in unto her, come back, neither attain they unto the paths of life:
text.KJV: None that go unto her return again, neither take they hold of the paths of life.
text.NABRE: None who enter there come back, or gain the paths of life.
text.DRB: All that go in unto her, shall not return neither shall they apprehend the paths of life.

text.NRSV: Therefore walk in the way of the good, and keep to the paths of the just.
text.Rotherham: To the end that thou walk in the way of good men, and, the paths of the righteous, that thou observe.
text.KJV: That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
text.NABRE: Thus you may walk in the way of the good, and keep to the paths of the just.
text.DRB: That thou mayst walk in a good way: and mayst keep the paths of the just.

text.NRSV: For the upright will abide in the land, and the innocent will remain in it;
text.Rotherham: For, the upright, shall abide on the earth,—and, the men of integrity, shall remain therein;
text.KJV: For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it.
text.NABRE: For the upright will dwell in the land, people of integrity will remain in it;
text.DRB: For they that are right, shall dwell in the earth, and the simple shall continue in it.

text.NRSV: but the wicked will be cut off from the land, and the treacherous will be rooted out of it.
text.Rotherham: But, the lawless, out of the earth, shall be cut off, and, traitors, shall they tear away therefrom.
text.KJV: But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it.
text.NABRE: But the wicked will be cut off from the land, the faithless will be rooted out of it.
text.DRB: But the impious shall be destroyed from the earth: and they that do unjustly shall be taken away from it.

text.NRSV: My child, do not forget my teaching, but let your heart keep my commandments;
text.Rotherham: My son, mine instruction, do not thou forget, and, my commandment, let thy heart observe;
text.KJV: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Confidence in God Leads to Prosperity My son, do not forget my teaching, take to heart my commands;
text.DRB: My son, forget not my law, and let thy heart keep my precepts.

text.NRSV: for length of days and years of life and abundant welfare they will give you.
text.Rotherham: For, length of days and years of life, and blessedness, shall they add to thee.
text.KJV: For length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee.
text.NABRE: For many days, and years of life, and peace, will they bring you.
text.DRB: For they shall add to thee length of days, and years of life, and peace.

text.NRSV: Do not let loyalty and faithfulness forsake you; bind them around your neck, write them on the tablet of your heart.
text.Rotherham: Lovingkindness and faithfulness, let them not forsake thee,—bind them, upon thy neck, write them upon the tablet of thy heart:
text.KJV: Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:
text.NABRE: Do not let love and fidelity forsake you; bind them around your neck; write them on the tablet of your heart.
text.DRB: Let not mercy and truth leave thee, put them about thy throat and write them in the tables of thy heart:

text.NRSV: So you will find favor and good repute in the sight of God and of people.
text.Rotherham: So find thou favour and good repute, in the eyes of God and man.
text.KJV: So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
text.NABRE: Then will you win favor and esteem before God and human beings.
text.DRB: and thou shalt find grace; and good discipline before God and men.

text.NRSV: Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not rely on your own insight.
text.Rotherham: Trust thou in Yahweh, with all thy heart, and, unto thine own understanding, do not lean:
text.KJV: Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
text.NABRE: Trust in the Lord with all your heart, on your own intelligence do not rely;
text.DRB: Have confidence in our Lord with all thy heart and lean not upon thine own prudence.

text.NRSV: In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.
text.Rotherham: In all thy ways, acknowledge him, and, he, will make straight thy paths.
text.KJV: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
text.NABRE: In all your ways be mindful of him, and he will make straight your paths.
text.DRB: In all thy ways think on him, and he will direct thy steps.

text.NRSV: Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the Lord, and turn away from evil.
text.Rotherham: Do not become wise in thine own eyes, revere Yahweh, and avoid evil:
text.KJV: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the Lord, and depart from evil.
text.NABRE: Do not be wise in your own eyes, fear the Lord and turn away from evil;
text.DRB: Be not wise in thine own conceit: fear God, and depart from evil:

text.NRSV: It will be a healing for your body and a refreshment for your bones.
text.Rotherham: Healing, shall it be to thy body, and refreshing, to thy bones.
text.KJV: It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones.
text.NABRE: This will mean health for your flesh and vigor for your bones.
text.DRB: for it shall be health to thy navel, and watering of thy bones.

text.NRSV: Honor the Lord with your substance and with the first fruits of all your produce;
text.Rotherham: Honour thou Yahweh, with thy substance, and with the firstfruit of all thine increase;
text.KJV: Honour the Lord with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:
text.NABRE: Honor the Lord with your wealth, with first fruits of all your produce;
text.DRB: Honour our Lord with thy substance, and give to him of the first of all thy fruits:

text.NRSV: then your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will be bursting with wine.
text.Rotherham: So shall thy storehouses be filled with plenty, and, with new wine, shall thy vats overflow.
text.KJV: So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.
text.NABRE: Then will your barns be filled with plenty, with new wine your vats will overflow.
text.DRB: and thy barns shall be replenished with fullness, and thy presses shall run-over with wine.

text.NRSV: My child, do not despise the Lord’s discipline or be weary of his reproof,
text.Rotherham: The, chastening of Yahweh, my son, do not reject, nor loathe his rebuke;
text.KJV: My son, despise not the chastening of the Lord; neither be weary of his correction:
text.NABRE: The discipline of the Lord, my son, do not spurn; do not disdain his reproof;
text.DRB: My son, cast not away the discipline of our Lord: neither do thou faint when thou art chastised of him:

text.NRSV: for the Lord reproves the one he loves, as a father the son in whom he delights.
text.Rotherham: For, whom Yahweh loveth, he correcteth, he causeth pain to the son in whom he delighteth.
text.KJV: For whom the Lord loveth he correcteth; even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.
text.NABRE: For whom the Lord loves he reproves, as a father, the son he favors.
text.DRB: for whom our Lord loveth he chastiseth: and as a father in the son he pleaseth himself.

text.NRSV: Happy are those who find wisdom, and those who get understanding,
text.Rotherham: How happy the man who hath found wisdom, and the man who draweth forth understanding,
text.KJV: Happy is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding.
text.NABRE: The Benefits of Finding Wisdom Happy the one who finds wisdom, the one who gains understanding!
text.DRB: Blessed is the man that findeth wisdom, and floweth with prudence:

text.NRSV: for her income is better than silver, and her revenue better than gold.
text.Rotherham: For better is her merchandise, than the merchandise of silver, yea, than gold, her increase;
text.KJV: For the merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.
text.NABRE: Her profit is better than profit in silver, and better than gold is her revenue;
text.DRB: better is the purchasing thereof than merchandise of silver, and her fruit than chief and the purest gold:

text.NRSV: She is more precious than jewels, and nothing you desire can compare with her.
text.Rotherham: More precious, is she, than corals, yea, none of thy delightful things, doth equal her:
text.KJV: She is more precious than rubies: and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.
text.NABRE: She is more precious than corals, and no treasure of yours can compare with her.
text.DRB: she is more precious than all riches: and all things that are desired, are not able to be compared with her.

text.NRSV: Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
text.Rotherham: Length of days, is in her right hand, in her left, are riches and honour;
text.KJV: Length of days is in her right hand; and in her left hand riches and honour.
text.NABRE: Long life is in her right hand, in her left are riches and honor;
text.DRB: Length of days in her right hand, & in her left hand riches and glory.

text.NRSV: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
text.Rotherham: Her ways, are ways of pleasantness, and, all her paths, are peace;
text.KJV: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
text.NABRE: Her ways are pleasant ways, and all her paths are peace;
text.DRB: Her ways are beautiful ways, and all her paths peaceable.

text.NRSV: She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; those who hold her fast are called happy.
text.Rotherham: A tree of life, is she, to them who secure her,—and, they who hold her fast, are every one to be pronounced happy.
text.KJV: She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her: and happy is every one that retaineth her.
text.NABRE: She is a tree of life to those who grasp her, and those who hold her fast are happy.
text.DRB: She is a tree of life to them that shall apprehend her: and he that shall hold her is blessed.

text.NRSV: The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, in wisdom, founded the earth, establishing the heavens with understanding;
text.KJV: The Lord by wisdom hath founded the earth; by understanding hath he established the heavens.
text.NABRE: The Lord by wisdom founded the earth, established the heavens by understanding;
text.DRB: Our Lord by wisdom foundeth the earth, established the heavens by prudence.

text.NRSV: by his knowledge the deeps broke open, and the clouds drop down the dew.
text.Rotherham: By his knowledge, the resounding deeps were burst open, and, the skies, drop down dew.
text.KJV: By his knowledge the depths are broken up, and the clouds drop down the dew.
text.NABRE: By his knowledge the depths are split, and the clouds drop down dew.
text.DRB: By his wisdom the depths have broken forth, and the clouds wax thick with dew.

text.NRSV: My child, do not let these escape from your sight: keep sound wisdom and prudence,
text.Rotherham: My son, let them not depart from thine eyes, guard thou counsel, and purpose:
text.KJV: My son, let not them depart from thine eyes: keep sound wisdom and discretion:
text.NABRE: Justice Toward One’s Neighbor Brings Blessing My son, do not let these slip from your sight: hold to deliberation and planning;
text.DRB: My son, let not these things depart from thine eyes: keep the law & counsel:

text.NRSV: and they will be life for your soul and adornment for your neck.
text.Rotherham: So shall they become life to thy soul, and an adorning to thy neck;
text.KJV: So shall they be life unto thy soul, and grace to thy neck.
text.NABRE: So will they be life to your soul, and an adornment for your neck.
text.DRB: and there shall be life to thy soul, and grace to thy jaws.

text.NRSV: Then you will walk on your way securely and your foot will not stumble.
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou walk securely in thy way, and, thy foot, shall not stumble;
text.KJV: Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.
text.NABRE: Then you may go your way securely; your foot will never stumble;
text.DRB: Then shalt thou walk confidently in thy way, and thy foot shall not stumble:

text.NRSV: If you sit down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
text.Rotherham: When thou sittest down, thou shalt have no dread, yea thou shalt lie down, and sweet shall be thy sleep.
text.KJV: When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.
text.NABRE: When you lie down, you will not be afraid, when you rest, your sleep will be sweet.
text.DRB: if thou sleep, thou shalt not fear: thou shalt rest, and thy sleep shall be sweet.

text.NRSV: Do not be afraid of sudden panic, or of the storm that strikes the wicked;
text.Rotherham: Be not thou afraid of sudden dread, nor of the desolation of the lawless, when it cometh.
text.KJV: Be not afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it cometh.
text.NABRE: Do not be afraid of sudden terror, of the ruin of the wicked when it comes;
text.DRB: Dread not at sudden terror, and the power of the impious falling upon thee.

text.NRSV: for the Lord will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, will be in all thy ways, and will keep thy foot from the snare.
text.KJV: For the Lord shall be thy confidence, and shall keep thy foot from being taken.
text.NABRE: For the Lord will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from the snare.
text.DRB: For our Lord will be at thy side, and will keep thy foot that thou be not taken.

text.NRSV: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in your power to do it.
text.Rotherham: Withhold not good from them who ask it, when it is in power of thy hand to do it:
text.KJV: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it.
text.NABRE: Do not withhold any goods from the owner when it is in your power to act.
text.DRB: Do not prohibit him to do good, that is able: if thou be able, thyself also do good.

text.NRSV: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again, tomorrow I will give it”—when you have it with you.
text.Rotherham: Do not say to thy neighbour, Go and come again, and to-morrow I will give, when thou hast it by thee.
text.KJV: Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and to morrow I will give; when thou hast it by thee.
text.NABRE: Say not to your neighbor, “Go, come back tomorrow, and I will give it to you,” when all the while you have it.
text.DRB: Say not to thy friend: go, and return; and tomorrow I will give to thee: whereas thou mayst give forthwith.

text.NRSV: Do not plan harm against your neighbor who lives trustingly beside you.
text.Rotherham: Devise not against thy neighbour an injury, seeing that, he, dwelleth securely by thee.
text.KJV: Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.
text.NABRE: Do not plot evil against your neighbors, when they live at peace with you.
text.DRB: Practise not evil against thy friend, when he hath affiance in thee.

text.NRSV: Do not quarrel with anyone without cause, when no harm has been done to you.
text.Rotherham: Contend not with a man without cause, if he hath dealt thee no wrong.
text.KJV: Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.
text.NABRE: Do not contend with someone without cause, with one who has done you no harm.
text.DRB: Contend not against man without cause, whereas he hath done thee no evil.

text.NRSV: Do not envy the violent and do not choose any of their ways;
text.Rotherham: Do not thou envy the man of violence, neither choose thou any of his ways;
text.KJV: Envy thou not the oppressor, and choose none of his ways.
text.NABRE: Do not envy the violent and choose none of their ways:
text.DRB: Do not envy an unjust man, nor imitate his ways:

text.NRSV: for the perverse are an abomination to the Lord, but the upright are in his confidence.
text.Rotherham: For, an abomination to Yahweh, is the tortuous man, but, with the upright, he is intimate.
text.KJV: For the froward is abomination to the Lord: but his secret is with the righteous.
text.NABRE: To the Lord the devious are an abomination, but the upright are close to him.
text.DRB: because every deluder is an abomination of our Lord, and his communication is with the simple.

text.NRSV: The Lord’s curse is on the house of the wicked, but he blesses the abode of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: The curse of Yahweh, is in the house of the lawless one, but, the home of the righteous, he blesseth.
text.KJV: The curse of the Lord is in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the just.
text.NABRE: The curse of the Lord is on the house of the wicked, but the dwelling of the just he blesses;
text.DRB: There is poverty from our Lord in the house of the impious: but the habitations of the just shall be blessed.

text.NRSV: Toward the scorners he is scornful, but to the humble he shows favor.
text.Rotherham: Though at scoffers he scoffeth, yet, to the humbled, he granteth favour.
text.KJV: Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the lowly.
text.NABRE: Those who scoff, he scoffs at, but the lowly he favors.
text.DRB: He shall delude the deluders, and to the mild he will give grace.

text.NRSV: The wise will inherit honor, but stubborn fools, disgrace.
text.Rotherham: Honour, shall the wise inherit,—but, as for dullards, shame shall carry them away.
text.KJV: The wise shall inherit glory: but shame shall be the promotion of fools.
text.NABRE: The wise will possess glory, but fools will bear shame.
text.DRB: The wise shall possess glory: the exaltation of fools ignominy.

text.NRSV: Listen, children, to a father’s instruction, and be attentive, that you may gain insight;
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye sons, the correction of a father, and attend, that ye may know understanding.
text.KJV: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know understanding.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 The Teacher as Model Disciple Hear, O children, a father’s instruction, be attentive, that you may gain understanding!
text.DRB: Children hear ye the father's discipline, and attend that you may know prudence.

text.NRSV: for I give you good precepts: do not forsake my teaching.
text.Rotherham: For, good teaching, have I given you, mine instruction, do not ye forsake.
text.KJV: For I give you good doctrine, forsake ye not my law.
text.NABRE: Yes, excellent advice I give you; my teaching do not forsake.
text.DRB: I will give you a good gift, forsake not my law.

text.NRSV: When I was a son with my father, tender, and my mother’s only child,
text.Rotherham: For, a son, became I to my father, tender and most precious in the sight of my mother.
text.KJV: For I was my father’s son, tender and only beloved in the sight of my mother.
text.NABRE: When I was my father’s child, tender, the darling of my mother,
text.DRB: For I also was the son of my father, tender and as the only-begotten in my mother's sight:

text.NRSV: he taught me, and said to me, “Let your heart hold fast my words; keep my commandments, and live.
text.Rotherham: So he taught me, and said to me—Let thy heart, lay hold of my words, Keep my commandments and live!
text.KJV: He taught me also, and said unto me, Let thine heart retain my words: keep my commandments, and live.
text.NABRE: He taught me and said to me: “Let your heart hold fast my words: keep my commands, and live!
text.DRB: and he taught me, & said: Let thy heart receive my words, keep my precepts, and thou shalt live.

text.NRSV: Get wisdom; get insight: do not forget, nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Acquire wisdom, acquire understanding, Do not forget, neither decline thou from the sayings of my mouth.
text.KJV: Get wisdom, get understanding: forget it not; neither decline from the words of my mouth.
text.NABRE: Get wisdom, get understanding! Do not forget or turn aside from the words of my mouth.
text.DRB: Possess wisdom, possess prudence: forget not, neither decline from the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV: Do not forsake her, and she will keep you; love her, and she will guard you.
text.Rotherham: Do not forsake her, and she will guard thee,—love her and she will keep thee.
text.KJV: Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.
text.NABRE: Do not forsake her, and she will preserve you; love her, and she will safeguard you;
text.DRB: Leave her not, and she will keep thee: love her, and she will preserve thee.

text.NRSV: The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom, and whatever else you get, get insight.
text.Rotherham: The principal thing, is wisdom, acquire thou wisdom, With all thine acquisition, acquire thou understanding.
text.KJV: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
text.NABRE: The beginning of wisdom is: get wisdom; whatever else you get, get understanding.
text.DRB: The beginning of wisdom, possess wisdom, and in all thy possession purchase prudence:

text.NRSV: Prize her highly, and she will exalt you; she will honor you if you embrace her.
text.Rotherham: Exalt her, and she will set thee on high, she will bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her:
text.KJV: Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.
text.NABRE: Extol her, and she will exalt you; she will bring you honors if you embrace her;
text.DRB: take her quickly, and she will exalt thee: thou shalt be glorified of her, when thou shalt embrace her.

text.NRSV: She will place on your head a fair garland; she will bestow on you a beautiful crown.”
text.Rotherham: She will give for thy head, a wreath of beauty, A crown of adorning, will she bestow upon thee.
text.KJV: She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee.
text.NABRE: She will put on your head a graceful diadem; a glorious crown will she bestow on you.”
text.DRB: She will give to thy head increase of graces, and with a noble crown she will protect thee.

text.NRSV: Hear, my child, and accept my words, that the years of your life may be many.
text.Rotherham: Hear, my son, and receive my sayings, and they will multiply to thee the years of life.
text.KJV: Hear, O my son, and receive my sayings; and the years of thy life shall be many.
text.NABRE: The Two Ways Hear, my son, and receive my words, and the years of your life shall be many.
text.DRB: Hear my son, and receive my words, that years of life may be multiplied to thee.

text.NRSV: I have taught you the way of wisdom; I have led you in the paths of uprightness.
text.Rotherham: In the way of wisdom, have I taught thee, I have guided thee in tracks of uprightness.
text.KJV: I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in right paths.
text.NABRE: On the way of wisdom I direct you, I lead you on straight paths.
text.DRB: The way of wisdom I will shew to thee, I will lead thee by the paths of equity.

text.NRSV: When you walk, your step will not be hampered; and if you run, you will not stumble.
text.Rotherham: When thou walkest, thy step shall not be hemmed in, and, if thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
text.KJV: When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
text.NABRE: When you walk, your step will not be impeded, and should you run, you will not stumble.
text.DRB: Which when thou shalt have entered, thy steps shall not be straitened, and running thou shalt not have a stumbling block.

text.NRSV: Keep hold of instruction; do not let go; guard her, for she is your life.
text.Rotherham: Take fast hold of correction, let her not go,—keep her, for, she, is thy life.
text.KJV: Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.
text.NABRE: Hold fast to instruction, never let it go; keep it, for it is your life.
text.DRB: Hold discipline, leave it not: keep it, because the same is thy life.

text.NRSV: Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not walk in the way of evildoers.
text.Rotherham: Upon the path of the lawless, do not thou enter, and do not advance in the way of the wicked:
text.KJV: Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil men.
text.NABRE: The path of the wicked do not enter, nor walk in the way of the evil;
text.DRB: Be not delighted in the paths of the impious, neither let the way of the evil please thee.

text.NRSV: Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.
text.Rotherham: Avoid it, do not pass thereon—turn from it, and depart.
text.KJV: Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away.
text.NABRE: Shun it, do not cross it, turn aside from it, pass on.
text.DRB: Fly from it, neither pass thou by it: go aside, and forsake it.

text.NRSV: For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong; they are robbed of sleep unless they have made someone stumble.
text.Rotherham: For they sleep not, unless they can do mischief,—They rob themselves of their sleep, if they cannot cause someone to stumble,
text.KJV: For they sleep not, except they have done mischief; and their sleep is taken away, unless they cause some to fall.
text.NABRE: For they cannot rest unless they have done evil; if they do not trip anyone they lose sleep.
text.DRB: For they sleep not unless they have done ill: and they take no sleep unless they supplant.

text.NRSV: For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
text.Rotherham: For they consume bread gotten by lawlessness, and, wine obtained by violence, they drink.
text.KJV: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.
text.NABRE: For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
text.DRB: They eat the bread of impiety, and drink the wine of iniquity.

text.NRSV: But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day.
text.Rotherham: But, the path of the righteous, is as the light of dawn,—going on and brightening, unto meridian day.
text.KJV: But the path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.
text.NABRE: But the path of the just is like shining light, that grows in brilliance till perfect day.
text.DRB: But the path of the just, as shining light, proceedeth even to perfect day.

text.NRSV: The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know what they stumble over.
text.Rotherham: The way of the lawless, is like darkness, they know not, at what they stumble.
text.KJV: The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.
text.NABRE: The way of the wicked is like darkness; they do not know on what they stumble.
text.DRB: The way of the impious is darksome: they know not where they fall.

text.NRSV: My child, be attentive to my words; incline your ear to my sayings.
text.Rotherham: My son, to my words, attend, to my sayings, incline thou thine ear;
text.KJV: My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.
text.NABRE: With Your Whole Being Heed My Words and Live My son, to my words be attentive, to my sayings incline your ear;
text.DRB: My son hear my words, and incline thine ear to my sayings.

text.NRSV: Do not let them escape from your sight; keep them within your heart.
text.Rotherham: Let them not depart from thine eyes, keep them in the midst of thy heart;
text.KJV: Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
text.NABRE: Let them not slip from your sight, keep them within your heart;
text.DRB: Let them not depart from thine eyes, keep them in the midst of thy heart.

text.NRSV: For they are life to those who find them, and healing to all their flesh.
text.Rotherham: For, life, they are, to them who find them,—and, to every part of one’s flesh, they bring healing.
text.KJV: For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.
text.NABRE: For they are life to those who find them, bringing health to one’s whole being.
text.DRB: For they are life to those that find them, and health to all flesh.

text.NRSV: Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.
text.Rotherham: Above all that must be guarded, keep thou thy heart, for, out of it, are the issues of life.
text.KJV: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
text.NABRE: With all vigilance guard your heart, for in it are the sources of life.
text.DRB: With all guard keep thy heart, because life proceedeth from it.

text.NRSV: Put away from you crooked speech, and put devious talk far from you.
text.Rotherham: Remove from thee, perverseness of mouth, and, craftiness of lips, put far from thee.
text.KJV: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee.
text.NABRE: Dishonest mouth put away from you, deceitful lips put far from you.
text.DRB: Remove from thee a froward mouth, and let detracting lips be far from thee.

text.NRSV: Let your eyes look directly forward, and your gaze be straight before you.
text.Rotherham: Let, thine eyes, right onward, look,—and, thine eyelashes, point straight before thee.
text.KJV: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
text.NABRE: Let your eyes look straight ahead and your gaze be focused forward.
text.DRB: Let thine eyes see right things, & let thine eye-lids go before thy steps.

text.NRSV: Keep straight the path of your feet, then all your ways will be sure.
text.Rotherham: Make level the track of thy foot, that, all thy ways, may be directed aright:
text.KJV: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
text.NABRE: Survey the path for your feet, and all your ways will be sure.
text.DRB: Direct the path to thy feet, and all thy ways shall be established.

text.NRSV: Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.
text.Rotherham: Decline not, to the right hand or to the left,—Turn away thy foot from wickedness.
text.KJV: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil.
text.NABRE: Turn neither to right nor to left, keep your foot far from evil.
text.DRB: Decline not to the right hand, nor to the left: turn away thy foot from evil. For our Lord knoweth the ways that are on the right hand: but those are perverse, which are on the left hand. But he will make thy courses right, and thy ways he will bring forward in peace.

text.NRSV: My child, be attentive to my wisdom; incline your ear to my understanding,
text.Rotherham: My son, to my wisdom, attend, and, to mine understanding, incline thou thine ear:
text.KJV: My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Warning Against Adultery My son, to my wisdom be attentive, to understanding incline your ear,
text.DRB: My son, attend to my wisdom, and to my prudence incline thine ear,

text.NRSV: so that you may hold on to prudence, and your lips may guard knowledge.
text.Rotherham: That thou mayest preserve discretion,—and, as for knowledge, that thy lips may guard it.
text.KJV: That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
text.NABRE: That you may act discreetly, and your lips guard what you know.
text.DRB: that thou mayst keep cogitations, and thy lips preserve discipline.

text.NRSV: For the lips of a loose woman drip honey, and her speech is smoother than oil;
text.Rotherham: For, with sweet droppings, drip the lips of her that is a stranger, and, smoother than oil, is her mouth;
text.KJV: For the lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb, and her mouth is smoother than oil:
text.NABRE: Indeed, the lips of the stranger drip honey, and her mouth is smoother than oil;
text.DRB: Attend not to the deceit of a woman: for the lips of an harlot are as a honey comb distilling, and her throat neater than oil.

text.NRSV: but in the end she is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.
text.Rotherham: But, the latter end of her, is bitter as wormwood, sharp, as a two-edged sword!
text.KJV: But her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword.
text.NABRE: But in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, as sharp as a two-edged sword.
text.DRB: But her later ends are bitter as wormwood, and sharp as a two edged sword.

text.NRSV: Her feet go down to death; her steps follow the path to Sheol;
text.Rotherham: Her feet, are going down to death,—on hades, will her steps take firm hold.
text.KJV: Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell.
text.NABRE: Her feet go down to death, her steps reach Sheol;
text.DRB: Her feet go down into death, and her steps penetrate unto hell.

text.NRSV: she does not keep to the smooth path of life; her steps wander, and she does not know it.
text.Rotherham: Lest, the path of life, she should ponder, her tracks have wandered she knoweth not [whither].
text.KJV: Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them.
text.NABRE: Her paths ramble, you know not where, lest you see before you the road to life.
text.DRB: They walk not by the path of life, her steps are wandering, and unsearchable.

text.NRSV: And now, my children, listen to me, and do not depart from the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye sons, hearken unto me, and do not turn away from the sayings of my mouth.
text.KJV: Hear me now therefore, O ye children, and depart not from the words of my mouth.
text.NABRE: So now, children, listen to me, do not stray from the words of my mouth.
text.DRB: Now therefore my son hear me, and depart not from the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV: Keep your way far from her, and do not go near the door of her house;
text.Rotherham: Keep far from her thy way, and do not go near the opening of her house:
text.KJV: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:
text.NABRE: Keep your way far from her, do not go near the door of her house,
text.DRB: Make thy way far from her, and approach not to the doors of her house.

text.NRSV: or you will give your honor to others, and your years to the merciless,
text.Rotherham: Lest thou give, to other men, thy vigour, and thy years, to him that is cruel:
text.KJV: Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel:
text.NABRE: Lest you give your honor to others, and your years to a merciless one;
text.DRB: Give not thy honour to strangers, and thy years to the cruel.

text.NRSV: and strangers will take their fill of your wealth, and your labors will go to the house of an alien;
text.Rotherham: Lest strange men, be well fed, by thy strength, and, thy toils, be in the house of the alien.
text.KJV: Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours be in the house of a stranger;
text.NABRE: Lest outsiders take their fill of your wealth, and your hard-won earnings go to another’s house;
text.DRB: Lest perhaps strangers be filled with thy strength, and thy labours be in another man's house,

text.NRSV: and at the end of your life you will groan, when your flesh and body are consumed,
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou grieve in thy latter end, in the failing of thy flesh and of thy healthy condition;
text.KJV: And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
text.NABRE: And you groan in the end, when your flesh and your body are consumed;
text.DRB: and thou mourn in the end, when thou shalt have spent thy flesh and thy body, and say:

text.NRSV: and you say, “Oh, how I hated discipline, and my heart despised reproof!
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say—How I hated correction! and, reproof, my heart disdained;
text.KJV: And say, How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;
text.NABRE: And you say, “Oh, why did I hate instruction, and my heart spurn reproof!
text.DRB: Why have I detested discipline, and my heart consented not to reprehensions,

text.NRSV: I did not listen to the voice of my teachers or incline my ear to my instructors.
text.Rotherham: Neither hearkened I to the voice of my teachers, nor, to my instructors, inclined I mine ear:
text.KJV: And have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor inclined mine ear to them that instructed me!
text.NABRE: Why did I not listen to the voice of my teachers, incline my ear to my instructors!
text.DRB: nor I heard the voice of them that taught me, and have not inclined mine ear to masters?

text.NRSV: Now I am at the point of utter ruin in the public assembly.”
text.Rotherham: Soon was I in all evil, in the midst of convocation and assembly.
text.KJV: I was almost in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.
text.NABRE: I am all but ruined, in the midst of the public assembly!”
text.DRB: I have almost been in all evil, in the midst of the Church and of the synagogue.

text.NRSV: Drink water from your own cistern, flowing water from your own well.
text.Rotherham: Drink thou water out of thine own cistern, and flowing streams out of the midst of thine own well.
text.KJV: Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well.
text.NABRE: Drink water from your own cistern, running water from your own well.
text.DRB: Drink water of thine own cistern, and the streams of thy well:

text.NRSV: Should your springs be scattered abroad, streams of water in the streets?
text.Rotherham: Let not thy fountains, flow over, abroad, in the streets, dividings of waters:
text.KJV: Let thy fountains be dispersed abroad, and rivers of waters in the streets.
text.NABRE: Should your water sources be dispersed abroad, streams of water in the streets?
text.DRB: Let thy fountains be derived abroad, and in the streets divide thy waters,

text.NRSV: Let them be for yourself alone, and not for sharing with strangers.
text.Rotherham: Let them be for thyself, alone, and not for strangers with thee.
text.KJV: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers’ with thee.
text.NABRE: Let them be yours alone, not shared with outsiders;
text.DRB: Have them alone, neither let strangers be partakers with thee.

text.NRSV: Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the wife of your youth,
text.Rotherham: Let thy well-spring be blessed,—and get thy joy from the wife of thy youth:—
text.KJV: Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
text.NABRE: Let your fountain be blessed and have joy of the wife of your youth,
text.DRB: Let thy vein be blessed, and rejoice with the woman of thy youth:

text.NRSV: a lovely hind, a graceful doe. May her breasts satisfy you at all times; may you be intoxicated always by her love.
text.Rotherham: A loving hind! a graceful doe! let, her bosom, content thee at all times, and, in her love, mayst thou stray evermore.
text.KJV: Let her be as the loving hind and pleasant roe; let her breasts satisfy thee at all times; and be thou ravished always with her love.
text.NABRE: your lovely hind, your graceful doe. Of whose love you will ever have your fill, and by her ardor always be intoxicated.
text.DRB: a hind most dear, and a most grateful fawn: let her breasts inebriate thee at all time, in her love be thou delighted continually.

text.NRSV: Why should you be intoxicated, my son, by another woman and embrace the bosom of an adulteress?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, shouldst thou stray, my son, with a strange woman? or embrace the bosom of a woman unknown?
text.KJV: And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?
text.NABRE: Why then, my son, should you be intoxicated with a stranger, and embrace another woman?
text.DRB: Why art thou seduced my son of a strange woman, and art cherished in the bosom of another?

text.NRSV: For human ways are under the eyes of the Lord, and he examines all their paths.
text.Rotherham: For, before the eyes of Yahweh, are the ways of a man,—and, all his tracks, doth he consider:
text.KJV: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the Lord, and he pondereth all his goings.
text.NABRE: Indeed, the ways of each person are plain to the Lord ’s sight; all their paths he surveys;
text.DRB: Our Lord beholdeth the ways of man, and considereth all his steps.

text.NRSV: The iniquities of the wicked ensnare them, and they are caught in the toils of their sin.
text.Rotherham: His own iniquities, shall entrap him with the lawless, and, by the cords of his own sin, shall he be seized.
text.KJV: His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins.
text.NABRE: By their own iniquities the wicked will be caught, in the meshes of their own sin they will be held fast;
text.DRB: His own iniquities take the impious, and he is fast bound with the ropes of his sins.

text.NRSV: They die for lack of discipline, and because of their great folly they are lost.
text.Rotherham: He, shall die, for lack of discipline, and, by the abounding of his perversity, shall he be lost.
text.KJV: He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
text.NABRE: They will die from lack of discipline, lost because of their great folly.
text.DRB: He shall die because he hath not had discipline, and in the multitude of his folly he shall be deceived.

text.NRSV: My child, if you have given your pledge to your neighbor, if you have bound yourself to a stranger,
text.Rotherham: My son, if thou have become surety for thy neighbour,—have struck for a stranger thy hands,
text.KJV: My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Miscellaneous Proverbs Against Going Surety for One’s Neighbor My son, if you have become surety to your neighbor, given your hand in pledge to another,
text.DRB: My son if thou shalt be surety for thy friend, and hast made fast thy hand to a stranger,

text.NRSV: you are snared by the utterance of your lips, caught by the words of your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast been snared by the sayings of thy mouth,—thou hast been caught by the sayings of thy mouth.
text.KJV: Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.
text.NABRE: You have been snared by the utterance of your lips, caught by the words of your mouth;
text.DRB: thou art entrapped with the words of thy mouth, & caught with thine own words.

text.NRSV: So do this, my child, and save yourself, for you have come into your neighbor’s power: go, hurry, and plead with your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Do this then, my son, and deliver thyself, When thou hast come into the hand of thy neighbour, Go, haste thee, and urge thy neighbour;
text.KJV: Do this now, my son, and deliver thyself, when thou art come into the hand of thy friend; go, humble thyself, and make sure thy friend.
text.NABRE: So do this, my son, to free yourself, since you have fallen into your neighbor’s power: Go, hurry, rouse your neighbor!
text.DRB: Do therefore my son that which I say, and deliver thyself, because thou art fallen into the hand of thy neighbour. Run diverse ways, make haste, raise thy friend.

text.NRSV: Give your eyes no sleep and your eyelids no slumber;
text.Rotherham: Do not give sleep to thine eyes, or slumber to thine eyelashes;
text.KJV: Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.
text.NABRE: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids;
text.DRB: Give not sleep to thine eyes, neither let thine eye-lids slumber.

text.NRSV: save yourself like a gazelle from the hunter, like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
text.Rotherham: Deliver thyself, as a gazelle out of the hand, and as a bird, out of the hand of the fowler.
text.KJV: Deliver thyself as a roe from the hand of the hunter, and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.
text.NABRE: Free yourself like a gazelle from the hunter, or like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
text.DRB: Deliver thyself as a little doe from the hand, and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.

text.NRSV: Go to the ant, you lazybones; consider its ways, and be wise.
text.Rotherham: Go to the ant, thou sluggard, observe her ways, and be wise;
text.KJV: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:
text.NABRE: The Ant and the Sluggard at Harvest Go to the ant, O sluggard, study her ways and learn wisdom;
text.DRB: Go to the emmot, o sluggard, and consider her ways, and learn wisdom.

text.NRSV: Without having any chief or officer or ruler,
text.Rotherham: Which, having no harvest, scribe, or ruler,
text.KJV: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,
text.NABRE: For though she has no chief, no commander or ruler,
text.DRB: Who whereas she hath no guide, nor master, nor captain,

text.NRSV: it prepares its food in summer, and gathers its sustenance in harvest.
text.Rotherham: Prepareth, in the summer, her food, hath collected, in the harvest, her sustenance.
text.KJV: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.
text.NABRE: She procures her food in the summer, stores up her provisions in the harvest.
text.DRB: prepareth meat for herself in the summer, and gathereth in the harvest for to eat.

text.NRSV: How long will you lie there, O lazybones? When will you rise from your sleep?
text.Rotherham: How long, O sluggard, wilt thou lie? how long ere thou rise from thy sleep?
text.KJV: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?
text.NABRE: How long, O sluggard, will you lie there? when will you rise from your sleep?
text.DRB: How long wilt thou sleep, o sluggard? When wilt thou rise out of thy sleep?

text.NRSV: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest,
text.Rotherham: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest:—
text.KJV: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:
text.NABRE: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the arms to rest—
text.DRB: Thou shalt sleep a little, a little shalt thou slumber, a little shalt thou join thy hands to sleep:

text.NRSV: and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like an armed warrior.
text.Rotherham: So shall come in, as a highwayman, thy poverty, and, thy want, as one armed with a shield.
text.KJV: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man.
text.NABRE: Then poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like a brigand.
text.DRB: and penury shall come to thee, as a wayfaring man, and poverty as a man armed. But if thou be not sluggish, thy harvest shall come as a fountain, and penury shall fly far from thee.

text.NRSV: A scoundrel and a villain goes around with crooked speech,
text.Rotherham: An abandoned man, a man of iniquity, [is he] who—goeth on in perversity of mouth;
text.KJV: A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.
text.NABRE: The Scoundrel Scoundrels, villains, are they who deal in crooked talk.
text.DRB: Aman that is an Apostata, a man unprofitable, goeth with perverse mouth,

text.NRSV: winking the eyes, shuffling the feet, pointing the fingers,
text.Rotherham: Winketh with his eyes, speaketh with his foot, pointeth with his fingers;
text.KJV: He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers;
text.NABRE: Shifty of eye, feet ever moving, pointing with fingers,
text.DRB: winketh with the eyes, treadeth with the foot, speaketh with the finger,

text.NRSV: with perverted mind devising evil, continually sowing discord;
text.Rotherham: [Hath] perverse things in his heart, deviseth mischief on every occasion, strifes, he sendeth forth.
text.KJV: Frowardness is in his heart, he deviseth mischief continually; he soweth discord.
text.NABRE: They have perversity in their hearts, always plotting evil, sowing discord.
text.DRB: with wicked heart he deviseth evil, and at all time he soweth brawls.

text.NRSV: therefore calamity will come upon them suddenly; in a moment they will be broken beyond healing.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, suddenly cometh his doom, in a moment, shall he be torn in pieces and there be no mending.
text.KJV: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.
text.NABRE: Therefore their doom comes suddenly; in an instant they are crushed beyond cure.
text.DRB: To him his destruction shall come forthwith, and he shall suddenly be destroyed, neither shall he have remedy any more.

text.NRSV: There are six things that the Lord hates, seven that are an abomination to him:
text.Rotherham: These six things, doth Yahweh hate, yea, seven, are the abomination of his soul:—
text.KJV: These six things doth the Lord hate: yea, seven are an abomination unto him:
text.NABRE: What the Lord Rejects There are six things the Lord hates, yes, seven are an abomination to him;
text.DRB: Six things there are which our Lord hateth, and the seventh his soul detesteth:

text.NRSV: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
text.Rotherham: Eyes that are lofty, a tongue that is false, and hands shedding innocent blood;
text.KJV: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
text.NABRE: Haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,
text.DRB: Lofty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,

text.NRSV: a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that hurry to run to evil,
text.Rotherham: A heart contriving iniquitous devices, feet hasting to run into mischief;
text.KJV: An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief,
text.NABRE: A heart that plots wicked schemes, feet that are quick to run to evil,
text.DRB: a heart that deviseth most wicked devices, feet swift to run into evil,

text.NRSV: a lying witness who testifies falsely, and one who sows discord in a family.
text.Rotherham: One that uttereth lies—a false witness, and one sending forth strifes between brethren.
text.KJV: A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.
text.NABRE: The false witness who utters lies, and the one who sows discord among kindred.
text.DRB: a deceitful witness that uttereth lies, and him that among brethren soweth discords.

text.NRSV: My child, keep your father’s commandment, and do not forsake your mother’s teaching.
text.Rotherham: Observe thou, my son, the commandment of thy father, and do not decline from the instruction of thy mother:
text.KJV: My son, keep thy father’s commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
text.NABRE: Warning Against Adultery Observe, my son, your father’s command, and do not reject your mother’s teaching;
text.DRB: My son keep the precepts of thy father, and leave not the law of thy mother.

text.NRSV: Bind them continually on your heart; tie them around your neck.
text.Rotherham: Bind them upon thy heart continually, fasten them upon thy neck;
text.KJV: Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.
text.NABRE: Keep them fastened over your heart always, tie them around your neck.
text.DRB: Bind them in thy heart continually, and put them about thy throat.

text.NRSV: When you walk, they will lead you; when you lie down, they will watch over you; and when you awake, they will talk with you.
text.Rotherham: When thou walkest abroad, it shall guide thee, when thou sleepest, it shall watch over thee, when thou wakest, it shall speak to thee:
text.KJV: When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.
text.NABRE: When you lie down they will watch over you, when you wake, they will share your concerns; wherever you turn, they will guide you.
text.DRB: When thou shalt walk, let them go with thee: when thou shalt sleep, let them keep thee, and awaking talk with them.

text.NRSV: For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light, and the reproofs of discipline are the way of life,
text.Rotherham: For, a lamp, is the commandment, and, the instruction, a light, and, the way of life, are the reproofs of correction:
text.KJV: For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life:
text.NABRE: For the command is a lamp, and the teaching a light, and a way to life are the reproofs that discipline,
text.DRB: Because the commandment is a lamp, and the law a light, and the way of life the increpation of discipline:

text.NRSV: to preserve you from the wife of another, from the smooth tongue of the adulteress.
text.Rotherham: To keep thee from the wicked woman, from the flattery of the tongue of her that is a stranger.
text.KJV: To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.
text.NABRE: Keeping you from another’s wife, from the smooth tongue of the foreign woman.
text.DRB: that they may keep thee from the evil woman, and from the fair spoken tongue of the stranger.

text.NRSV: Do not desire her beauty in your heart, and do not let her capture you with her eyelashes;
text.Rotherham: Do not covet her beauty, in thy heart, neither let her take thee, by her eyelashes;
text.KJV: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
text.NABRE: Do not lust in your heart after her beauty, do not let her captivate you with her glance!
text.DRB: Let not thy heart covet her beauty, be not caught with her becks:

text.NRSV: for a prostitute’s fee is only a loaf of bread, but the wife of another stalks a man’s very life.
text.Rotherham: Because, for the sake of an impure woman, [a man may be brought] even to a cake of bread,—and, a man’s wife, for a precious soul, may hunt!
text.KJV: For by means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread: and the adulteress will hunt for the precious life.
text.NABRE: For the price of a harlot may be scarcely a loaf of bread, But a married woman is a trap for your precious life.
text.DRB: for the price of an harlot is scarce worth one loaf: but a woman catcheth the precious soul of man.

text.NRSV: Can a man carry fire in his bosom and his clothes not be burned?
text.Rotherham: Can a man snatch up fire in his bosom, and, his clothes, not be burned?
text.KJV: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his clothes not be burned?
text.NABRE: Can a man take embers into his bosom, and his garments not be burned?
text.DRB: Can a man hide fire in his bosom, that his garments burn not?

text.NRSV: Or can one walk on hot coals and his feet not be scorched?
text.Rotherham: Or can a man walk upon hot coals, and, his feet, not be burned?
text.KJV: Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned?
text.NABRE: Or can a man walk on live coals, and his feet not be scorched?
text.DRB: Or walk upon hot coals, that his soles be not burnt?

text.NRSV: So is he who sleeps with his neighbor’s wife; no one who touches her will go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: So, he that goeth in unto his neighbour’s wife, no man shall be guiltless who toucheth her!
text.KJV: So he that goeth in to his neighbour’s wife; whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.
text.NABRE: So with him who sleeps with another’s wife— none who touches her shall go unpunished.
text.DRB: so he that goeth in unto his neighbour's wife, shall not be clean when he shall touch her.

text.NRSV: Thieves are not despised who steal only to satisfy their appetite when they are hungry.
text.Rotherham: Men despise not a thief, when he stealeth, to satisfy his appetite, because he is famished;
text.KJV: Men do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry;
text.NABRE: Thieves are not despised if out of hunger they steal to satisfy their appetite.
text.DRB: It is no great fault, when a man shall have stolen: for he stealeth to fill his hungry soul:
