text.NRSV: Yet if they are caught, they will pay sevenfold; they will forfeit all the goods of their house.
text.Rotherham: Yet, if found, he must pay back sevenfold, All the substance of his house, must he give:
text.KJV: But if he be found, he shall restore sevenfold; he shall give all the substance of his house.
text.NABRE: Yet if caught they must pay back sevenfold, yield up all the wealth of their house.
text.DRB: also being taken he shall restore sevenfold, and shall give up all the substance of his house.

text.NRSV: But he who commits adultery has no sense; he who does it destroys himself.
text.Rotherham: He that committeth adultery with a woman, lacketh sense, A destroyer of his own life, is he that doeth it;
text.KJV: But whoso committeth adultery with a woman lacketh understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.
text.NABRE: But those who commit adultery have no sense; those who do it destroy themselves.
text.DRB: But he that is an adulterer, for penury of heart shall destroy his own soul:

text.NRSV: He will get wounds and dishonor, and his disgrace will not be wiped away.
text.Rotherham: Smiting and shame, shall he find, and, his reproach, shall not be wiped out;
text.KJV: A wound and dishonour shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.
text.NABRE: They will be beaten and disgraced, and their shame will not be wiped away;
text.DRB: shame and ignominy he gathereth to himself, & his reproach shall not be blotted out.

text.NRSV: For jealousy arouses a husband’s fury, and he shows no restraint when he takes revenge.
text.Rotherham: For, jealousy, is the rage of a man, nor will he spare, in the day of avenging;
text.KJV: For jealousy is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
text.NABRE: For passion enrages the husband, he will have no pity on the day of vengeance;
text.DRB: Because the zeal and fury of the husband will not spare in the day of revenge,

text.NRSV: He will accept no compensation, and refuses a bribe no matter how great.
text.Rotherham: He will not look, at any ransom, neither will he consent, though thou increase the bribe.
text.KJV: He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.
text.NABRE: He will not consider any restitution, nor be satisfied by your many bribes.
text.DRB: neither will he yield to any man's prayers, neither will he take for redemption very many gifts.

text.NRSV: My child, keep my words and store up my commandments with you;
text.Rotherham: My son, keep my sayings, and, my commandments, treasure by thee;
text.KJV: My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 The Seduction My son, keep my words, and treasure my commands.
text.DRB: My son keep my words, and my precepts hide with thee. Son,

text.NRSV: keep my commandments and live, keep my teachings as the apple of your eye;
text.Rotherham: Keep my commandments and live, and mine instruction, as the pupil of thine eye;
text.KJV: Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.
text.NABRE: Keep my commands and live, and my teaching as the apple of your eye;
text.DRB: keep my commandments, and thou shalt live: and my law as the apple of thine eye:

text.NRSV: bind them on your fingers, write them on the tablet of your heart.
text.Rotherham: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them, on the tablet of thy heart;
text.KJV: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.
text.NABRE: Bind them on your fingers, write them on the tablet of your heart.
text.DRB: bind it on thy fingers, write it in the tables of thy heart.

text.NRSV: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and call insight your intimate friend,
text.Rotherham: Say unto wisdom, My sister, thou! and, an acquaintance, call thou, understanding:
text.KJV: Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister; and call understanding thy kinswoman:
text.NABRE: Say to Wisdom, “You are my sister!” Call Understanding, “Friend!”
text.DRB: Say to wisdom, thou art my sister: & call prudence thy friend,

text.NRSV: that they may keep you from the loose woman, from the adulteress with her smooth words.
text.Rotherham: That thou mayest be kept, from the woman that is a stranger, from the female unknown, who, with her speeches, doth flatter.
text.KJV: That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.
text.NABRE: That they may keep you from a stranger, from the foreign woman with her smooth words.
text.DRB: that she may keep thee from the strange woman, and from the foreigner which maketh her words sweet.

text.NRSV: For at the window of my house I looked out through my lattice,
text.Rotherham: For, in the window of my house, through my lattice, I looked out;
text.KJV: For at the window of my house I looked through my casement,
text.NABRE: For at the window of my house, through my lattice I looked out
text.DRB: For out of the window of my house I looked out through the lattice,

text.NRSV: and I saw among the simple ones, I observed among the youths, a young man without sense,
text.Rotherham: And saw among the simple ones, discerned among the youths, A young man lacking sense;
text.KJV: And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,
text.NABRE: And I saw among the naive, I observed among the young men, a youth with no sense,
text.DRB: and I see little ones, I behold a foolish youngman,

text.NRSV: passing along the street near her corner, taking the road to her house
text.Rotherham: Passing through the street, near her corner, and, on the way to her house, he sauntered along;
text.KJV: Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,
text.NABRE: Crossing the street near the corner, then walking toward her house,
text.DRB: which passeth through the streets by the corner, and goeth nigh the way of her house,

text.NRSV: in the twilight, in the evening, at the time of night and darkness.
text.Rotherham: In the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the midst of the night, and the gloom;
text.KJV: In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:
text.NABRE: In the twilight, at dusk of day, in the very dark of night.
text.DRB: in the dark the day being toward evening, in the darkness of the night, and dimness.

text.NRSV: Then a woman comes toward him, decked out like a prostitute, wily of heart.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a woman, came to meet him, attired as one unchaste, of a wily heart.
text.KJV: And, behold, there met him a woman with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart.
text.NABRE: Then the woman comes to meet him, dressed like a harlot, with secret designs.
text.DRB: And behold the woman meeteth him in harlot's attire, prepared to deceive souls: babbling and wandering,

text.NRSV: She is loud and wayward; her feet do not stay at home;
text.Rotherham: Boisterous, is she, and rebellious, In her house, abide not her feet;
text.KJV: (She is loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house:
text.NABRE: She is raucous and unruly, her feet cannot stay at home;
text.DRB: impatient of rest, nor able to consist in the house on her feet,

text.NRSV: now in the street, now in the squares, and at every corner she lies in wait.
text.Rotherham: Now outside, now in the broadways, and, near every corner, she lieth in wait:
text.KJV: Now is she without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)
text.NABRE: Now she is in the streets, now in the open squares, lurking in ambush at every corner.
text.DRB: now abroad, now in the streets, now lying in wait near the corners.

text.NRSV: She seizes him and kisses him, and with impudent face she says to him:
text.Rotherham: So she caught him, and kissed him, and, embolding her face, she said to him:
text.KJV: So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him,
text.NABRE: Then she grabs him, kisses him, and with an impudent look says to him:
text.DRB: And taking the youngman she kisseth him, and with malapert countenance speaketh fair, saying:

text.NRSV: “I had to offer sacrifices, and today I have paid my vows;
text.Rotherham: Peace-offerings, are by me, to-day, have I paid my vows;
text.KJV: I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.
text.NABRE: “I owed peace offerings, and today I have fulfilled my vows;
text.DRB: I vowed victims for welfare, this day I have payed my vows.

text.NRSV: so now I have come out to meet you, to seek you eagerly, and I have found you!
text.Rotherham: For this cause, came I forth to meet thee, to seek diligently thy face, and I have found thee:
text.KJV: Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee.
text.NABRE: So I came out to meet you, to look for you, and I have found you!
text.DRB: Therefore I am come forth to meet thee, desirous to see, and I have found thee.

text.NRSV: I have decked my couch with coverings, colored spreads of Egyptian linen;
text.Rotherham: Coverlets, have I spread on my couch of pleasure, dark-hued stuffs, of the yarn of Egypt;
text.KJV: I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved works, with fine linen of Egypt.
text.NABRE: With coverlets I have spread my couch, with brocaded cloths of Egyptian linen;
text.DRB: I have woven my bed with cords, I have adorned it with tapestry pictured out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
text.Rotherham: I have sprinkled my bed, with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon:
text.KJV: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
text.NABRE: I have sprinkled my bed with myrrh, with aloes, and with cinnamon.
text.DRB: I have sprinkled my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

text.NRSV: Come, let us take our fill of love until morning; let us delight ourselves with love.
text.Rotherham: Come! let us take our fill of endearments, until morning, let us delight ourselves with caresses;
text.KJV: Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.
text.NABRE: Come, let us drink our fill of love, until morning, let us feast on love!
text.DRB: Come let us be inebriated with breasts, and let us enjoy desired embracings, till the day appear.

text.NRSV: For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
text.Rotherham: For the husband is not in his house, he hath gone on a journey afar;
text.KJV: For the goodman is not at home, he is gone a long journey:
text.NABRE: For my husband is not at home, he has gone on a long journey;
text.DRB: For my husband is not at home, he is gone a very long journey.

text.NRSV: He took a bag of money with him; he will not come home until full moon.”
text.Rotherham: A bag of silver, hath he taken in his hand, On the day of the full moon, will he enter his house.
text.KJV: He hath taken a bag of money with him, and will come home at the day appointed.
text.NABRE: A bag of money he took with him, he will not return home till the full moon.”
text.DRB: he carried with him a bag of money: in the day of the full moon he will return to his house.

text.NRSV: With much seductive speech she persuades him; with her smooth talk she compels him.
text.Rotherham: She turneth him aside, with her great persuasiveness,—with the flattery of her lips, she compelleth him:
text.KJV: With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.
text.NABRE: She wins him over by repeated urging, with her smooth lips she leads him astray.
text.DRB: She entangled him with many words, and with flattery of lips drew him.

text.NRSV: Right away he follows her, and goes like an ox to the slaughter, or bound like a stag to the trap
text.Rotherham: Going after her instantly, as an ox, to the slaughter, he entereth, and, as in fetters, unto the correction of a fool.
text.KJV: He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks;
text.NABRE: He follows her impulsively, like an ox that goes to slaughter; Like a stag that bounds toward the net,
text.DRB: Immediately he followeth her as an oxe led to be a victim, & as a lamb playing the wanton, and not knowing that he is drawn as a fool to bonds,

text.NRSV: until an arrow pierces its entrails. He is like a bird rushing into a snare, not knowing that it will cost him his life.
text.Rotherham: Until an arrow cleaveth his liver, as a bird hasteth into a snare, and knoweth not, that, for his life, it is!
text.KJV: Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.
text.NABRE: till an arrow pierces its liver; Like a bird that rushes into a snare, unaware that his life is at stake.
text.DRB: till the arrow pierce his liver: as if a bird should make haste to the snare, and knoweth not that his life is in danger.

text.NRSV: And now, my children, listen to me, and be attentive to the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye sons, hearken unto me, and attend, to the sayings of my mouth;
text.KJV: Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
text.NABRE: So now, children, listen to me, be attentive to the words of my mouth!
text.DRB: Now therefore my son, hear me, and attend to the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV: Do not let your hearts turn aside to her ways; do not stray into her paths;
text.Rotherham: Let not thy heart, turn aside to her ways, Do not go astray, in her paths.
text.KJV: Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.
text.NABRE: Do not let your heart turn to her ways, do not go astray in her paths;
text.DRB: Let not thy mind be drawn away in her ways: neither be thou deceived with her paths.

text.NRSV: for many are those she has laid low, and numerous are her victims.
text.Rotherham: For, many, wounded, hath she caused to fall,—yea strong men, slain wholly by her:
text.KJV: For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her.
text.NABRE: For many are those she has struck down dead, numerous, those she has slain.
text.DRB: For she hath cast down many wounded, and all the most strong are slain by her.

text.NRSV: Her house is the way to Sheol, going down to the chambers of death.
text.Rotherham: Ways to hades, are in her house, descending into the chambers of death.
text.KJV: Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.
text.NABRE: Her house is a highway to Sheol, leading down into the chambers of death.
text.DRB: Her house the ways of hell, penetrating to the inner parts of the death.

text.NRSV: Does not wisdom call, and does not understanding raise her voice?
text.Rotherham: Doth not, wisdom, cry aloud? and, understanding, send forth her voice?
text.KJV: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding put forth her voice?
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 The Discourse of Wisdom Does not Wisdom call, and Understanding raise her voice?
text.DRB: Doth not wisdom cry, and prudence give her voice?

text.NRSV: On the heights, beside the way, at the crossroads she takes her stand;
text.Rotherham: At the top of the high places above the way, at the place where paths meet, she taketh her stand:
text.KJV: She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths.
text.NABRE: On the top of the heights along the road, at the crossroads she takes her stand;
text.DRB: Standing in the high & lofty tops over the way, in the midst of the paths,

text.NRSV: beside the gates in front of the town, at the entrance of the portals she cries out:
text.Rotherham: Beside the gates, at the entrance of the city,—at the going in of the openings, she shouteth:—
text.KJV: She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.
text.NABRE: By the gates at the approaches of the city, in the entryways she cries aloud:
text.DRB: beside the gates of the city in the very doors she speaketh, saying:

text.NRSV: “To you, O people, I call, and my cry is to all that live.
text.Rotherham: Unto you, O men, I call, and, my voice, is unto the sons of men;
text.KJV: Unto you, O men, I call; and my voice is to the sons of man.
text.NABRE: “To you, O people, I call; my appeal is to you mortals.
text.DRB: O men, to you I cry, and my voice is to the children of men.

text.NRSV: O simple ones, learn prudence; acquire intelligence, you who lack it.
text.Rotherham: Understand, ye simple ones, shrewdness, and, ye dullards understand sense;
text.KJV: O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.
text.NABRE: You naive ones, gain prudence, you fools, gain sense.
text.DRB: O little ones understand subtilty, and ye unwise mark.

text.NRSV: Hear, for I will speak noble things, and from my lips will come what is right;
text.Rotherham: Hear, for, princely things, will I speak, and the opening of my lips shall be of equity;
text.KJV: Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.
text.NABRE: Listen! for noble things I speak; my lips proclaim honest words.
text.DRB: Hear ye, because I will speak of great things: and my lips shall be opened to preach right things.

text.NRSV: for my mouth will utter truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
text.Rotherham: For, faithfulness, shall my mouth softly utter, but, the abomination of my lips, shall be lawlessness;
text.KJV: For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
text.NABRE: Indeed, my mouth utters truth, and my lips abhor wickedness.
text.DRB: My throat shall meditate truth, and my lips shall detest the impious.

text.NRSV: All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
text.Rotherham: In righteousness, shall be all the sayings of my mouth, nothing therein, shall be crafty or perverse;
text.KJV: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing froward or perverse in them.
text.NABRE: All the words of my mouth are sincere, none of them wily or crooked;
text.DRB: All my words are just, there is no wicked, nor perverse thing in them.

text.NRSV: They are all straight to one who understands and right to those who find knowledge.
text.Rotherham: All of them, shall be plain, to them who would understand, and just, to such as would gain knowledge.
text.KJV: They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.
text.NABRE: All of them are straightforward to the intelligent, and right to those who attain knowledge.
text.DRB: They are right to them that understand, and just to them that find knowledge.

text.NRSV: Take my instruction instead of silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold;
text.Rotherham: Receive my correction, and not silver, and knowledge, rather than choicest gold.
text.KJV: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.
text.NABRE: Take my instruction instead of silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold.
text.DRB: Receive ye discipline, & not money: choose doctrine rather than gold.

text.NRSV: for wisdom is better than jewels, and all that you may desire cannot compare with her.
text.Rotherham: For better is wisdom, than ornaments of coral, and, no delightful things, can equal her.
text.KJV: For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.
text.NABRE: [For Wisdom is better than corals, and no treasures can compare with her. ]
text.DRB: For wisdom is better than all most precious riches: and whatsoever is to be desired cannot be compared to it.

text.NRSV: I, wisdom, live with prudence, and I attain knowledge and discretion.
text.Rotherham: I, wisdom, inhabit shrewdness,—and, the knowledge of sagacious things, I gain.
text.KJV: I wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions.
text.NABRE: I, Wisdom, dwell with prudence, and useful knowledge I have.
text.DRB: I wisdom dwell in counsel, and am present in learned cogitations.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is to hate wickedness: pride, arrogance, and the way of wickedness; And a mouth of perverse things, do I hate.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.
text.NABRE: [The fear of the Lord is hatred of evil;] Pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the perverse mouth I hate.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord hateth evil: arrogancy, and pride, and wicked way, and a double tongued mouth I do detest.

text.NRSV: I have good advice and sound wisdom; I have insight, I have strength.
text.Rotherham: Mine, are counsel, and effective working, I, am understanding, mine, is valour:
text.KJV: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength.
text.NABRE: Mine are counsel and advice; Mine is strength; I am understanding.
text.DRB: Mine is counsel & equity, prudence is mine, strength is mine.

text.NRSV: By me kings reign, and rulers decree what is just;
text.Rotherham: By me, kings reign, and dignitaries decree righteousness;
text.KJV: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.
text.NABRE: By me kings reign, and rulers enact justice;
text.DRB: By me kings do reign, and the makers of laws decree just things.

text.NRSV: by me rulers rule, and nobles, all who govern rightly.
text.Rotherham: By me, rulers govern, and nobles—all the righteous judges:
text.KJV: By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth.
text.NABRE: By me princes govern, and nobles, all the judges of the earth.
text.DRB: By me Princes rule, and the mighty decree justice.

text.NRSV: I love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently find me.
text.Rotherham: I love, them who love me, and, they who diligently seek me, find me:
text.KJV: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me.
text.NABRE: Those who love me I also love, and those who seek me find me.
text.DRB: I love them that love me: and they that watch toward me shall find me.

text.NRSV: Riches and honor are with me, enduring wealth and prosperity.
text.Rotherham: Riches and honour, are with me, lordly wealth, and righteousness;
text.KJV: Riches and honour are with me; yea, durable riches and righteousness.
text.NABRE: With me are riches and honor, wealth that endures, and righteousness.
text.DRB: With me are riches, and glory, glorious riches, and justice.

text.NRSV: My fruit is better than gold, even fine gold, and my yield than choice silver.
text.Rotherham: Better is my fruit, than gold—yea fine gold, and mine increase, than choice silver;
text.KJV: My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.
text.NABRE: My fruit is better than gold, even pure gold, and my yield than choice silver.
text.DRB: For my fruit is better than gold and precious stone, and my blossoms than chosen silver.

text.NRSV: I walk in the way of righteousness, along the paths of justice,
text.Rotherham: In the way of righteousness, I march along, in the middle of the paths of justice:
text.KJV: I lead in the way of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of judgment:
text.NABRE: On the way of righteousness I walk, along the paths of justice,
text.DRB: I walk in the ways of justice, in the midst of the paths of judgment,

text.NRSV: endowing with wealth those who love me, and filling their treasuries.
text.Rotherham: That I may cause them who love me to inherit substance, and, their treasuries, I may fill.
text.KJV: That I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasures.
text.NABRE: Granting wealth to those who love me, and filling their treasuries.
text.DRB: that I may enrich them that love me, and may replenish their treasures.

text.NRSV: The Lord created me at the beginning of his work, the first of his acts of old.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, had constituted me the beginning of his way, before his works, at the commencement of that time;
text.KJV: The Lord possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.
text.NABRE: “The Lord begot me, the beginning of his works, the forerunner of his deeds of long ago;
text.DRB: Our Lord possessed me in the beginning of his ways, before he had made any thing from the beginning.

text.NRSV: Ages ago I was set up, at the first, before the beginning of the earth.
text.Rotherham: At the outset of the ages, had I been established, in advance of the antiquities of the earth;
text.KJV: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was.
text.NABRE: From of old I was formed, at the first, before the earth.
text.DRB: From eternity I was ordained & of old before the earth was made.

text.NRSV: When there were no depths I was brought forth, when there were no springs abounding with water.
text.Rotherham: When there was no resounding deep, I had been brought forth, when there were no fountains, abounding with water;
text.KJV: When there were no depths, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains abounding with water.
text.NABRE: When there were no deeps I was brought forth, when there were no fountains or springs of water;
text.DRB: The depths were not as yet, and I was now conceived, neither had the fountains of waters as yet gushed forth:

text.NRSV: Before the mountains had been shaped, before the hills, I was brought forth—
text.Rotherham: Ere yet the mountains had been settled, before the hills, had I been brought forth;
text.KJV: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth:
text.NABRE: Before the mountains were settled into place, before the hills, I was brought forth;
text.DRB: the mountains with heavy hugeness stood not as yet: before the little hills I was brought forth:

text.NRSV: when he had not yet made earth and fields, or the world’s first bits of soil.
text.Rotherham: Or ever he had made the land and the wastes, or the top of the dry parts of the world:
text.KJV: While as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the highest part of the dust of the world.
text.NABRE: When the earth and the fields were not yet made, nor the first clods of the world.
text.DRB: he had not yet made the earth, and the rivers, and the poles of the round world.

text.NRSV: When he established the heavens, I was there, when he drew a circle on the face of the deep,
text.Rotherham: When he prepared the heavens, there, was I! When he decreed a vault upon the face of the resounding deep;
text.KJV: When he prepared the heavens, I was there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth:
text.NABRE: When he established the heavens, there was I, when he marked out the vault over the face of the deep;
text.DRB: When he prepared the heavens, I was present: when with a certain law and circuit he compassed the depths:

text.NRSV: when he made firm the skies above, when he established the fountains of the deep,
text.Rotherham: When he made firm the skies above, when the fountains of the resounding deep, waxed strong;
text.KJV: When he established the clouds above: when he strengthened the fountains of the deep:
text.NABRE: When he made firm the skies above, when he fixed fast the springs of the deep;
text.DRB: When he established the firmament above, & poised the fountains of waters:

text.NRSV: when he assigned to the sea its limit, so that the waters might not transgress his command, when he marked out the foundations of the earth,
text.Rotherham: When he fixed for the sea its bound, that, the waters, should not go beyond his bidding, when he decreed the foundations of the earth:—
text.KJV: When he gave to the sea his decree, that the waters should not pass his commandment: when he appointed the foundations of the earth:
text.NABRE: When he set for the sea its limit, so that the waters should not transgress his command; When he fixed the foundations of earth,
text.DRB: when he compassed the sea with her limits, and set a law to the waters that they should not pass their bounds: when he hanged the foundations of the earth,

text.NRSV: then I was beside him, like a master worker; and I was daily his delight, rejoicing before him always,
text.Rotherham: Then became I beside him, a firm and sure worker, then became I filled with delight, day by day, exulting before him on every occasion;
text.KJV: Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;
text.NABRE: then was I beside him as artisan; I was his delight day by day, playing before him all the while,
text.DRB: I was with him framing all things: and was delighted every day, playing before him at all time,

text.NRSV: rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the human race.
text.Rotherham: Exulting in the fruitful land of his earth, Yea, my fulness of delight, was with the sons of men.
text.KJV: Rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth; and my delights were with the sons of men.
text.NABRE: Playing over the whole of his earth, having my delight with human beings.
text.DRB: playing in the world: & my delights to be with the children of men.

text.NRSV: And now, my children, listen to me: happy are those who keep my ways.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye sons, hearken to me, for how happy are they who, to my ways, pay regard!
text.KJV: Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways.
text.NABRE: Now, children, listen to me; happy are they who keep my ways.
text.DRB: Now therefore children hear me: Blessed are they that keep my ways.

text.NRSV: Hear instruction and be wise, and do not neglect it.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye correction, and be wise, and do not neglect.
text.KJV: Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.
text.NABRE: Listen to instruction and grow wise, do not reject it!
text.DRB: Hear ye discipline, and be wise, and reject it not.

text.NRSV: Happy is the one who listens to me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at my doorposts.
text.Rotherham: How happy the man that doth hearken to me,—keeping guard at my doors, day by day, watching at the posts of my gates;
text.KJV: Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.
text.NABRE: Happy the one who listens to me, attending daily at my gates, keeping watch at my doorposts;
text.DRB: Blessed is the man that heareth me, & that watcheth at my doors daily, & waiteth at the posts of my door.

text.NRSV: For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the Lord;
text.Rotherham: For, he that findeth me, findeth life, and hath obtained favour from Yahweh;
text.KJV: For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the Lord.
text.NABRE: For whoever finds me finds life, and wins favor from the Lord;
text.DRB: He that shall find me shall find life, and shall draw salvation of our Lord:

text.NRSV: but those who miss me injure themselves; all who hate me love death.”
text.Rotherham: But, he that misseth me, wrongeth his own soul, all who hate me, love death.
text.KJV: But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: all they that hate me love death.
text.NABRE: But those who pass me by do violence to themselves; all who hate me love death.”
text.DRB: but he that shall sin against me, shall hurt his own soul. All that hate me, love death.

text.NRSV: Wisdom has built her house, she has hewn her seven pillars.
text.Rotherham: Wisdom, hath builded her house, hath hewn out her seven pillars;
text.KJV: Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars:
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 The Two Women Invite Passersby to Their Banquets Woman Wisdom Issues Her Invitation Wisdom has built her house, she has set up her seven columns;
text.DRB: Wisdom hath built herself an house, she hath cut out seven pillars.

text.NRSV: She has slaughtered her animals, she has mixed her wine, she has also set her table.
text.Rotherham: hath slaughtered her beasts, hath mingled her wine, hath even set in order her table;
text.KJV: She hath killed her beasts; she hath mingled her wine; she hath also furnished her table.
text.NABRE: She has prepared her meat, mixed her wine, yes, she has spread her table.
text.DRB: She hath immolated her victims, mingled her wine, and set forth her table.

text.NRSV: She has sent out her servant-girls, she calls from the highest places in the town,
text.Rotherham: hath sent forth her maidens, She crieth aloud, upon the tops of the heights of the city:
text.KJV: She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city,
text.NABRE: She has sent out her maidservants; she calls from the heights out over the city:
text.DRB: She hath sent her hand-maids to call to the tower, and to the walls of the city:

text.NRSV: “You that are simple, turn in here!” To those without sense she says,
text.Rotherham: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither, as for him that lacketh sense, she saith to him—
text.KJV: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,
text.NABRE: “Let whoever is naive turn in here; to any who lack sense I say,
text.DRB: If any be a little one, let him come to me. And to the unwise she spake:

text.NRSV: “Come, eat of my bread and drink of the wine I have mixed.
text.Rotherham: Come, feed on my food, and drink of the wine I have mingled;
text.KJV: Come, eat of my bread, and drink of the wine which I have mingled.
text.NABRE: Come, eat of my food, and drink of the wine I have mixed!
text.DRB: Come, eat ye my bread, & drink the wine which I have mingled for you.

text.NRSV: Lay aside immaturity, and live, and walk in the way of insight.”
text.Rotherham: Forsake the simple ones, and live, and advance in the way of understanding.
text.KJV: Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding.
text.NABRE: Forsake foolishness that you may live; advance in the way of understanding.”
text.DRB: Leave infancy, and live, and walk by the ways of prudence.

text.NRSV: Whoever corrects a scoffer wins abuse; whoever rebukes the wicked gets hurt.
text.Rotherham: He that rebuketh a scoffer, getteth to himself contempt, and, he that reproveth a lawless man, [getteth to himself] his shame.
text.KJV: He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked man getteth himself a blot.
text.NABRE: Miscellaneous Aphorisms Whoever corrects the arrogant earns insults; and whoever reproves the wicked incurs opprobrium.
text.DRB: He that teacheth a scorner doth injury to himself; and he that rebuketh the impious, purchaseth a blot to himself.

text.NRSV: A scoffer who is rebuked will only hate you; the wise, when rebuked, will love you.
text.Rotherham: Do not reprove a scoffer, lest he hate thee, Reprove a wise man, and he will love thee.
text.KJV: Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.
text.NABRE: Do not reprove the arrogant, lest they hate you; reprove the wise, and they will love you.
text.DRB: Rebuke not the scorner lest he hate thee. Rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.

text.NRSV: Give instruction to the wise, and they will become wiser still; teach the righteous and they will gain in learning.
text.Rotherham: Give to a wise man, and he will be wiser still, Inform a righteous man, and he will increase learning.
text.KJV: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.
text.NABRE: Instruct the wise, and they become still wiser; teach the just, and they advance in learning.
text.DRB: Give occasion to a wise man, and wisdom shall be added to him. Teach the just, and he shall make haste to take it.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight.
text.Rotherham: The beginning of wisdom, is the reverence of Yahweh, and, the knowledge of the Holy, is understanding;
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
text.NABRE: The beginning of wisdom is fear of the Lord, and knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
text.DRB: The beginning of wisdom, the fear of our Lord: and the knowledge of the holy, prudence.

text.NRSV: For by me your days will be multiplied, and years will be added to your life.
text.Rotherham: For, by me, shall be multiplied—thy days, and there be added to thee—the years of life.
text.KJV: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased.
text.NABRE: For by me your days will be multiplied and the years of your life increased.
text.DRB: For by me shall thy days be multiplied, and years of life shall be added to thee.

text.NRSV: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself; if you scoff, you alone will bear it.
text.Rotherham: If thou art wise, thou art wise for thyself, but, if thou scoff, alone, shalt thou bear it.
text.KJV: If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it.
text.NABRE: If you are wise, wisdom is to your advantage; if you are arrogant, you alone shall bear it.
text.DRB: If thou be wise, to thyself thou shalt be: and if a scorner, thou alone shalt bear the evil.

text.NRSV: The foolish woman is loud; she is ignorant and knows nothing.
text.Rotherham: The woman Stupidity, is boisterous, so simple that she knoweth not what she would do;
text.KJV: A foolish woman is clamorous: she is simple, and knoweth nothing.
text.NABRE: Woman Folly Issues Her Invitation Woman Folly is raucous, utterly foolish; she knows nothing.
text.DRB: Afoolish woman and clamorous, and full of allurements, and knowing nothing at all,

text.NRSV: She sits at the door of her house, on a seat at the high places of the town,
text.Rotherham: So she sitteth at the entrance of her house, upon a seat, in the heights of the city;
text.KJV: For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,
text.NABRE: She sits at the door of her house upon a seat on the city heights,
text.DRB: sat in the doors of her house upon a seat in a high place of the city,

text.NRSV: calling to those who pass by, who are going straight on their way,
text.Rotherham: To invite them who pass by the way, who are going straight on in their paths:
text.KJV: To call passengers who go right on their ways:
text.NABRE: Calling to passersby as they go on their way straight ahead:
text.DRB: to call them that pass by the way, and go on their journey:

text.NRSV: “You who are simple, turn in here!” And to those without sense she says,
text.Rotherham: Whoso is simple, let him turn aside hither, and, as for him that lacketh sense, she saith to him:
text.KJV: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: and as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,
text.NABRE: “Let those who are naive turn in here, to those who lack sense I say,
text.DRB: he that is a little one, let him turn to me. And to the fool she spake:

text.NRSV: “Stolen water is sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
text.Rotherham: Stolen waters, will be sweet,—and a secret meal, will be pleasant;
text.KJV: Stolen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.
text.NABRE: Stolen water is sweet, and bread taken secretly is pleasing!”
text.DRB: Stolen waters are sweeter, and hidden bread more pleasant.

text.NRSV: But they do not know that the shades are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
text.Rotherham: But he knoweth not, that the shades are there; In the depths of hades, are her guests.
text.KJV: But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell.
text.NABRE: Little do they know that the shades are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol!
text.DRB: And he was ignorant that giants are there, and her guests in the depths of hell.

text.NRSV: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise child makes a glad father, but a foolish child is a mother’s grief.
text.Rotherham: The Proverbs of Solomon: A wise son, maketh a glad father, but, a foolish son, is the grief of his mother.
text.KJV: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.
text.NABRE: III. First Solomonic Collection of Sayings Chapter 10 The Proverbs of Solomon: A wise son gives his father joy, but a foolish son is a grief to his mother.
text.DRB: Awise son maketh the father glad: but a foolish son is the sorrow of his mother.

text.NRSV: Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit, but righteousness delivers from death.
text.Rotherham: The treasures of lawlessness, do not profit, but, righteousness, delivereth from death.
text.KJV: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.
text.NABRE: Ill-gotten treasures profit nothing, but justice saves from death.
text.DRB: The treasures of impiety shall profit nothing: but justice shall deliver from death.

text.NRSV: The Lord does not let the righteous go hungry, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, suffereth not to famish, the soul of the righteous, but, the desire of the lawless, he thrusteth away.
text.KJV: The Lord will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked.
text.NABRE: The Lord does not let the just go hungry, but the craving of the wicked he thwarts.
text.DRB: Our Lord will not afflict with famine the soul of the just, and the deceitful practices of the impious he will overthrow.

text.NRSV: A slack hand causes poverty, but the hand of the diligent makes rich.
text.Rotherham: He becometh poor, who dealeth with a slack hand, but, the hand of the diligent, maketh rich.
text.KJV: He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
text.NABRE: The slack hand impoverishes, but the busy hand brings riches.
text.DRB: The slothful hand hath wrought poverty: the hand of the strong getteth riches. Who so trusteth to lies feedeth the winds: and the self-same man followeth the flying birds.

text.NRSV: A son who gathers in summer is prudent, but a son who sleeps in harvest brings shame.
text.Rotherham: He that gathereth in summer, is a prudent son, he that sleepeth long in harvest, is a son causing shame.
text.KJV: He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.
text.NABRE: A son who gathers in summer is a credit; a son who slumbers during harvest, a disgrace.
text.DRB: He that gathereth in the harvest, is a wise son: but he that snorteth in summer, is the son of confusion.

text.NRSV: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
text.Rotherham: Blessings, are for the head of the righteous man, but, the mouth of the lawless, covereth up wrong.
text.KJV: Blessings are upon the head of the just: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
text.NABRE: Blessings are for the head of the just; but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
text.DRB: The blessing of our Lord is upon the head of the just: but iniquity covereth the mouth of the impious.

text.NRSV: The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.
text.Rotherham: The memory of the righteous, yieldeth blessing, but, the name of the lawless, dieth out.
text.KJV: The memory of the just is blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot.
text.NABRE: The memory of the just serves as blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.
text.DRB: The memory of the just is with praises: and the name of the impious shall rot.

text.NRSV: The wise of heart will heed commandments, but a babbling fool will come to ruin.
text.Rotherham: The wise in heart, will accept commandments, but, he that is foolish with his lips, shall be thrust away.
text.KJV: The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.
text.NABRE: A wise heart accepts commands, but a babbling fool will be overthrown.
text.DRB: The wise of heart shall receive precepts: a fool is beaten with lips.

text.NRSV: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, but whoever follows perverted ways will be found out.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh uprightly, may walk securely, but, he that maketh crooked his ways, shall be found out.
text.KJV: He that walketh uprightly walketh surely: but he that perverteth his ways shall be known.
text.NABRE: Whoever walks honestly walks securely, but one whose ways are crooked will fare badly.
text.DRB: He that walketh plainly, walketh confidently: but he that depraveth his ways, shall be manifest.

text.NRSV: Whoever winks the eye causes trouble, but the babbling fool will come to ruin.
text.Rotherham: He that winketh with the eye, causeth sorrow, and, he that is foolish with his lips, shall be thrust aside.
text.KJV: He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.
text.NABRE: One who winks at a fault causes trouble, but one who frankly reproves promotes peace.
text.DRB: He that winketh with the eye, shall give sorrow: and the fool in lips shall be beaten.

text.NRSV: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
text.Rotherham: A well-spring of life, is the mouth of the righteous, but, the mouth of the lawless, covereth wrong.
text.KJV: The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
text.NABRE: The mouth of the just is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
text.DRB: Avein of life, the mouth of the just: and the mouth of the impious covereth iniquity.

text.NRSV: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses.
text.Rotherham: Hatred, stirreth up strifes, but, over all transgressions, love throweth a covering.
text.KJV: Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.
text.NABRE: Hatred stirs up disputes, but love covers all offenses.
text.DRB: Hatred raiseth brawls: and charity covereth all sins.

text.NRSV: On the lips of one who has understanding wisdom is found, but a rod is for the back of one who lacks sense.
text.Rotherham: In the lips of the intelligent, is found wisdom, but, a rod, is for the back of him that lacketh sense.
text.KJV: In the lips of him that hath understanding wisdom is found: but a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding.
text.NABRE: On the lips of the intelligent is found wisdom, but a rod for the back of one without sense.
text.DRB: In the lips of the wise wisdom is found: and a rod on his back that lacketh wit.

text.NRSV: The wise lay up knowledge, but the babbling of a fool brings ruin near.
text.Rotherham: Wise men, treasure up knowledge, but, the mouth of the foolish, is a terror near at hand.
text.KJV: Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
text.NABRE: The wise store up knowledge, but the mouth of a fool is imminent ruin.
text.DRB: Wise men hide knowledge: but the mouth of the fool is next to confusion.

text.NRSV: The wealth of the rich is their fortress; the poverty of the poor is their ruin.
text.Rotherham: The substance of the rich, is his strong city, the terror of the poor, is their poverty.
text.KJV: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city: the destruction of the poor is their poverty.
text.NABRE: The wealth of the rich is their strong city; the ruin of the poor is their poverty.
text.DRB: The substance of a rich man, is a city of his strength: the fear of the poor their poverty.

text.NRSV: The wage of the righteous leads to life, the gain of the wicked to sin.
text.Rotherham: The labour of the righteous, [leadeth] to life, the increase of the lawless, to sin.
text.KJV: The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.
text.NABRE: The labor of the just leads to life, the gains of the wicked, to futility.
text.DRB: The work of the just unto life: but the fruit of the impious unto sin.

text.NRSV: Whoever heeds instruction is on the path to life, but one who rejects a rebuke goes astray.
text.Rotherham: On the way to life, is he that heedeth correction, but, he that hateth reproof, is going astray.
text.KJV: He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction: but he that refuseth reproof erreth.
text.NABRE: Whoever follows instruction is in the path to life, but whoever disregards reproof goes astray.
text.DRB: The way of life, to him that keepeth discipline: but he that forsaketh reprehensions, erreth.

text.NRSV: Lying lips conceal hatred, and whoever utters slander is a fool.
text.Rotherham: He that concealeth hatred, hath false lips, and he that sendeth forth slander, the same, is a dullard.
text.KJV: He that hideth hatred with lying lips, and he that uttereth a slander, is a fool.
text.NABRE: Whoever conceals hatred has lying lips, and whoever spreads slander is a fool.
text.DRB: Lying lips hide hatred: he that uttereth contumely is unwise.

text.NRSV: When words are many, transgression is not lacking, but the prudent are restrained in speech.
text.Rotherham: In the multitude of words, there wanteth not transgression, but, he that restraineth his lips, sheweth prudence.
text.KJV: In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin: but he that refraineth his lips is wise.
text.NABRE: Where words are many, sin is not wanting; but those who restrain their lips do well.
text.DRB: In much talk there shall not want sin: but he that moderateth his lips is most wise.

text.NRSV: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver; the mind of the wicked is of little worth.
text.Rotherham: Choice silver, is the tongue of the righteous, but, the sense of the lawless, is very small.
text.KJV: The tongue of the just is as choice silver: the heart of the wicked is little worth.
text.NABRE: Choice silver is the tongue of the just; the heart of the wicked is of little worth.
text.DRB: The tongue of the just, is chosen silver: but the heart of the impious is nothing worth.

text.NRSV: The lips of the righteous feed many, but fools die for lack of sense.
text.Rotherham: The lips of the righteous, feed multitudes, but, the foolish, for lack of sense, shall die.
text.KJV: The lips of the righteous feed many: but fools die for want of wisdom.
text.NABRE: The lips of the just nourish many, but fools die for want of sense.
text.DRB: The lips of the just teach very many: but they that are untaught, shall die in the penury of wit.

text.NRSV: The blessing of the Lord makes rich, and he adds no sorrow with it.
text.Rotherham: The blessing of Yahweh, itself maketh rich, and he addeth no grievance therewith.
text.KJV: The blessing of the Lord, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.
text.NABRE: It is the Lord ’s blessing that brings wealth, and no effort can substitute for it.
text.DRB: The blessing of our Lord maketh men rich: neither shall affliction be joined to them.

text.NRSV: Doing wrong is like sport to a fool, but wise conduct is pleasure to a person of understanding.
text.Rotherham: It is, mere sport to a stupid man, to commit lewdness, but, wisdom, pertaineth to a man of understanding.
text.KJV: It is as sport to a fool to do mischief: but a man of understanding hath wisdom.
text.NABRE: Crime is the entertainment of the fool; but wisdom is for the person of understanding.
text.DRB: Afool worketh mischief as it were by laughter: but wisdom is prudence to a man.

text.NRSV: What the wicked dread will come upon them, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.
text.Rotherham: The dread of the lawless one, the same, shall overtake him, but, the desire of the righteous, shall he granted.
text.KJV: The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.
text.NABRE: What the wicked fear will befall them, but the desire of the just will be granted.
text.DRB: That which the impious feareth, shall come upon him: to the just their desire shall be given.

text.NRSV: When the tempest passes, the wicked are no more, but the righteous are established forever.
text.Rotherham: Like the passing away of a tempest, so the lawless one is not, but, the righteous, [hath] an age-abiding foundation.
text.KJV: As the whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an everlasting foundation.
text.NABRE: When the tempest passes, the wicked are no more; but the just are established forever.
text.DRB: As a tempest passing the impious shall not be: but the just as an everlasting foundation.

text.NRSV: Like vinegar to the teeth, and smoke to the eyes, so are the lazy to those who send them.
text.Rotherham: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so, is the sluggard, to them who send him.
text.KJV: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him.
text.NABRE: As vinegar to the teeth, and smoke to the eyes, are sluggards to those who send them.
text.DRB: As vinegar to the teeth, and smoke to the eyes, so a sluggard to them that sent him.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord prolongs life, but the years of the wicked will be short.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, addeth days, but, the years of the lawless, shall be shortened.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord prolongeth days: but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
text.NABRE: Fear of the Lord prolongs life, but the years of the wicked are cut short.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord shall add days: and the years of the impious shall be shortened.

text.NRSV: The hope of the righteous ends in gladness, but the expectation of the wicked comes to nothing.
text.Rotherham: The hope of the righteous, shall be gladness, but, the expectation of the lawless, shall vanish.
text.KJV: The hope of the righteous shall be gladness: but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.
text.NABRE: The hope of the just brings joy, but the expectation of the wicked perishes.
text.DRB: The expectation of the just is joy: but the hope of the impious shall perish.

text.NRSV: The way of the Lord is a stronghold for the upright, but destruction for evildoers.
text.Rotherham: A refuge for the blameless, is the path of Yahweh, but, destruction, [awaiteth] the workers of iniquity.
text.KJV: The way of the Lord is strength to the upright: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.
text.NABRE: The Lord is a stronghold to those who walk honestly, downfall for evildoers.
text.DRB: The strength of the simple the way of our Lord: and fear is to them that work evil.

text.NRSV: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not inhabit the land.
text.Rotherham: The righteous, to times age-abiding, shall remain unshaken, but, the lawless, shall not inhabit the earth.
text.KJV: The righteous shall never be removed: but the wicked shall not inhabit the earth.
text.NABRE: The just will never be disturbed, but the wicked will not abide in the land.
text.DRB: The just for ever shall not be moved: but the impious shall not dwell on the earth.

text.NRSV: The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
text.Rotherham: The mouth of the righteous, beareth the fruit of wisdom, but, a perverse tongue, shall be cut off.
text.KJV: The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out.
text.NABRE: The mouth of the just yields wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
text.DRB: The mouth of the just shall bring forth wisdom: the tongue of the froward shall perish.

text.NRSV: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked what is perverse.
text.Rotherham: The lips of the righteous, know what is pleasing, but, the mouth of the lawless, [speaketh] perversities.
text.KJV: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.
text.NABRE: The lips of the just know favor, but the mouth of the wicked, perversion.
text.DRB: The lips of the just consider grateful things: and the mouth of the impious perverse things.

text.NRSV: A false balance is an abomination to the Lord, but an accurate weight is his delight.
text.Rotherham: A deceptive balance, is an abomination to Yahweh, but, a full weight, is his delight.
text.KJV: A false balance is abomination to the Lord: but a just weight is his delight.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 False scales are an abomination to the Lord, but an honest weight, his delight.
text.DRB: Adeceitful balance, is abomination before God: and an equal weight is his will.

text.NRSV: When pride comes, then comes disgrace; but wisdom is with the humble.
text.Rotherham: When pride cometh, then cometh contempt, but, with the modest, is wisdom.
text.KJV: When pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly is wisdom.
text.NABRE: When pride comes, disgrace comes; but with the humble is wisdom.
text.DRB: Where pride shall be, there shall be contumely also: but where humility, there also wisdom.

text.NRSV: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.
text.Rotherham: The integrity of the upright, shall guide them, but, the crookedness of the treacherous, shall be their ruin.
text.KJV: The integrity of the upright shall guide them: but the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them.
text.NABRE: The honesty of the upright guides them; the faithless are ruined by their duplicity.
text.DRB: The simplicity of the just shall direct them: and the supplanting of the perverse shall waste them.

text.NRSV: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
text.Rotherham: Wealth, shall not profit, in the day of wrath, but, righteousness, shall deliver from death.
text.KJV: Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death.
text.NABRE: Wealth is useless on a day of wrath, but justice saves from death.
text.DRB: Riches shall not profit in the day of revenge: but justice shall deliver from death.

text.NRSV: The righteousness of the blameless keeps their ways straight, but the wicked fall by their own wickedness.
text.Rotherham: The righteousness of the blameless, shall smooth his way, but, by his own lawlessness, shall the lawless one, fall.
text.KJV: The righteousness of the perfect shall direct his way: but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.
text.NABRE: The justice of the honest makes their way straight, but by their wickedness the wicked fall.
text.DRB: The justice of the simple shall direct his way: and the impious shall fall in his impiety.

text.NRSV: The righteousness of the upright delivers them, but the treacherous are taken captive by their schemes.
text.Rotherham: The righteousness of the upright, shall deliver them, but, by their own craving, shall the treacherous be captured.
text.KJV: The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them: but transgressors shall be taken in their own naughtiness.
text.NABRE: The justice of the upright saves them, but the faithless are caught in their own intrigue.
text.DRB: The justice of the righteous shall deliver them: and the unjust shall be caught in their deceitful practices.

text.NRSV: When the wicked die, their hope perishes, and the expectation of the impious comes to nothing.
text.Rotherham: When the lawless man dieth, his expectation, perisheth, and, the hope of strong men, hath vanished.
text.KJV: When a wicked man dieth, his expectation shall perish: and the hope of unjust men perisheth.
text.NABRE: When a person dies, hope is destroyed; expectation pinned on wealth is destroyed.
text.DRB: The impious man being dead, there shall be no hope any more: and the expectation of the careful shall perish.

text.NRSV: The righteous are delivered from trouble, and the wicked get into it instead.
text.Rotherham: The righteous man, out of distress, is delivered, then cometh a lawless man into his place.
text.KJV: The righteous is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked cometh in his stead.
text.NABRE: The just are rescued from a tight spot, but the wicked fall into it instead.
text.DRB: The just is delivered from distress: and the impious shall be given for him.

text.NRSV: With their mouths the godless would destroy their neighbors, but by knowledge the righteous are delivered.
text.Rotherham: With the mouth, a profane man destroyeth his neighbour, but, through knowledge, shall righteous men be delivered.
text.KJV: An hypocrite with his mouth destroyeth his neighbour: but through knowledge shall the just be delivered.
text.NABRE: By a word the impious ruin their neighbors, but through their knowledge the just are rescued.
text.DRB: The dissembler with his mouth deceiveth his friend: but the just shall be delivered by knowledge.

text.NRSV: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices; and when the wicked perish, there is shouting.
text.Rotherham: When it is, well with the righteous, the city, exulteth, When the lawless perish, there is a shout of triumph.
text.KJV: When it goeth well with the righteous, the city rejoiceth: and when the wicked perish, there is shouting.
text.NABRE: When the just prosper, the city rejoices; when the wicked perish, there is jubilation.
text.DRB: In the good things of the just the city shall rejoice; and in the destruction of the impious there shall be praise.

text.NRSV: By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: When the upright are blessed, exalted is the city, but, by the mouth of the lawless, it is overthrown.
text.KJV: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted: but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
text.NABRE: Through the blessing of the upright the city is exalted, but through the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown.
text.DRB: With the benediction of the just the city shall be exalted: and by the mouth of the impious it shall be subverted.

text.NRSV: Whoever belittles another lacks sense, but one who has understanding remains silent.
text.Rotherham: He that sheweth contempt for his neighbour, lacketh sense, but, a man of understanding, observeth silence.
text.KJV: He that is void of wisdom despiseth his neighbour: but a man of understanding holdeth his peace.
text.NABRE: Whoever reviles a neighbor lacks sense, but the intelligent keep silent.
text.DRB: He that despiseth his friend, lacketh heart: but the wise man will hold his peace.

text.NRSV: A gossip goes about telling secrets, but one who is trustworthy in spirit keeps a confidence.
text.Rotherham: He that goeth about talebearing, revealeth a secret, but, he that is faithful in spirit, concealeth a matter.
text.KJV: A talebearer revealeth secrets: but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter.
text.NABRE: One who slanders reveals secrets, but a trustworthy person keeps a confidence.
text.DRB: He that walketh fraudulently, revealeth secrets: but he that is faithful, concealeth the thing committed of his friend.

text.NRSV: Where there is no guidance, a nation falls, but in an abundance of counselors there is safety.
text.Rotherham: With no guidance, a people will fall, but, safety, lieth in the greatness of the counsellor.
text.KJV: Where no counsel is, the people fall: but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety.
text.NABRE: For lack of guidance a people falls; security lies in many counselors.
text.DRB: Where there is no governor, the people shall fall: but there is health where is much counsel.

text.NRSV: To guarantee loans for a stranger brings trouble, but whoever refuses to shake hands on a guarantee is safe.
text.Rotherham: He that becometh surety for a stranger, goeth to utter ruin, but, he that hateth striking hands, is secure.
text.KJV: He that is surety for a stranger shall smart for it: and he that hateth suretiship is sure.
text.NABRE: Harm will come to anyone going surety for another, but whoever hates giving pledges is secure.
text.DRB: He shall be afflicted with evil, that is surety for a stranger: but he that is ware of the snares, shall be secure.

text.NRSV: A gracious woman gets honor, but she who hates virtue is a throne of disgrace. The timid become destitute, but the aggressive gain riches.
text.Rotherham: A gracious wife, obtaineth honour, but, the diligent, shall obtain wealth.
text.KJV: A gracious woman retaineth honour: and strong men retain riches.
text.NABRE: A gracious woman gains esteem, and ruthless men gain wealth.
text.DRB: Agracious woman shall find glory: and the strong shall have riches.

text.NRSV: Those who are kind reward themselves, but the cruel do themselves harm.
text.Rotherham: The man of lovingkindness, dealeth well with his own soul, but the cruel man, troubleth his own flesh.
text.KJV: The merciful man doeth good to his own soul: but he that is cruel troubleth his own flesh.
text.NABRE: Kindly people benefit themselves, but the merciless harm themselves.
text.DRB: A merciful man doth good to his kindred also. Benefacit animæ suæ vir misericors; qui autem crudelis est, etiam propinquos abjicit.

text.NRSV: The wicked earn empty wages, but those who sow righteousness receive a true reward.
text.Rotherham: The lawless man, earneth the wages of falsehood, but, he that soweth righteousness, hath the reward of fidelity.
text.KJV: The wicked worketh a deceitful work: but to him that soweth righteousness shall be a sure reward.
text.NABRE: The wicked make empty profits, but those who sow justice have a sure reward.
text.DRB: The impious maketh an unstable work: but to him that soweth justice, is a faithful reward.

text.NRSV: Whoever is steadfast in righteousness will live, but whoever pursues evil will die.
text.Rotherham: A righteous son, [turneth] to life, but, he that pursueth wickedness, to his own death.
text.KJV: As righteousness tendeth to life: so he that pursueth evil pursueth it to his own death.
text.NABRE: Justice leads toward life, but pursuit of evil, toward death.
text.DRB: Clemency prepareth life: & the pursuing of evil things death.

text.NRSV: Crooked minds are an abomination to the Lord, but those of blameless ways are his delight.
text.Rotherham: The abomination of Yahweh, are they of perverse heart, but, his delight, are the men of blameless life.
text.KJV: They that are of a froward heart are abomination to the Lord: but such as are upright in their way are his delight.
text.NABRE: The crooked in heart are an abomination to the Lord, but those who walk blamelessly are his delight.
text.DRB: Aperverse heart is abominable to our Lord: and his will is in them that walk simply.

text.NRSV: Be assured, the wicked will not go unpunished, but those who are righteous will escape.
text.Rotherham: Hand to hand, the wicked man shall not be held innocent, but, the seed of the righteous, hath been delivered.
text.KJV: Though hand join in hand, the wicked shall not be unpunished: but the seed of the righteous shall be delivered.
text.NABRE: Be assured, the wicked shall not go unpunished, but the offspring of the just shall escape.
text.DRB: Hand in hand the evil man shall not be innocent: but the seed of the just shall be saved.

text.NRSV: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout is a beautiful woman without good sense.
text.Rotherham: As a ring of gold in the snout of a swine, is a woman of beauty who hath abandoned discretion.
text.KJV: As a jewel of gold in a swine’s snout, so is a fair woman which is without discretion.
text.NABRE: Like a golden ring in a swine’s snout is a beautiful woman without judgment.
text.DRB: Aring of gold in a swine's snout, a fair woman & a fool.

text.NRSV: The desire of the righteous ends only in good; the expectation of the wicked in wrath.
text.Rotherham: The desire of the righteous, is only good, the expectation of the lawless, is wrath.
text.KJV: The desire of the righteous is only good: but the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
text.NABRE: The desire of the just ends only in good; the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
text.DRB: The desire of the just is all good: the expectation of the impious fury.

text.NRSV: Some give freely, yet grow all the richer; others withhold what is due, and only suffer want.
text.Rotherham: There is who scattereth, and increaseth yet more, and who withholdeth of what is due, only to come to want.
text.KJV: There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and there is that withholdeth more than is meet, but it tendeth to poverty.
text.NABRE: One person is lavish yet grows still richer; another is too sparing, yet is the poorer.
text.DRB: Some divide their own goods, and are made richer: others take violently not their own, and are always in poverty.

text.NRSV: A generous person will be enriched, and one who gives water will get water.
text.Rotherham: The liberal soul, shall be enriched, and, he that refresheth, shall himself also be refreshed.
text.KJV: The liberal soul shall be made fat: and he that watereth shall be watered also himself.
text.NABRE: Whoever confers benefits will be amply enriched, and whoever refreshes others will be refreshed.
text.DRB: The soul which blesseth, shall be made fat: and he that inebriateth himself shall also be inebriated.

text.NRSV: The people curse those who hold back grain, but a blessing is on the head of those who sell it.
text.Rotherham: He that keepeth back corn, the populace will curse him, but there is, a blessing, for the head of him that selleth.
text.KJV: He that withholdeth corn, the people shall curse him: but blessing shall be upon the head of him that selleth it.
text.NABRE: Whoever hoards grain, the people curse, but blessings are on the head of one who distributes it!
text.DRB: He that hideth corn, shall be cursed among the peoples: but blessing upon the head of them that sell.

text.NRSV: Whoever diligently seeks good seeks favor, but evil comes to the one who searches for it.
text.Rotherham: He that diligently seeketh good, aimeth at favour, but, he that studieth mischief, it shall come on himself.
text.KJV: He that diligently seeketh good procureth favour: but he that seeketh mischief, it shall come unto him.
text.NABRE: Those who seek the good seek favor, but those who pursue evil will have evil come upon them.
text.DRB: Well riseth he early who seeketh good things: but he that is a searcher after evil things, shall be oppressed of them.

text.NRSV: Those who trust in their riches will wither, but the righteous will flourish like green leaves.
text.Rotherham: He that trusteth in his riches, the same, shall fall, but, like the leaf, shall the righteous break forth.
text.KJV: He that trusteth in his riches shall fall: but the righteous shall flourish as a branch.
text.NABRE: Those who trust in their riches will fall, but like green leaves the just will flourish.
text.DRB: He that trusteth in his riches shall fall: but the just shall spring as a green leaf.

text.NRSV: Those who trouble their households will inherit wind, and the fool will be servant to the wise.
text.Rotherham: He that troubleth his own house, shall inherit the wind, but, a servant, shall the foolish be, to the wise in heart.
text.KJV: He that troubleth his own house shall inherit the wind: and the fool shall be servant to the wise of heart.
text.NABRE: Those who trouble their household inherit the wind, and fools become slaves to the wise of heart.
text.DRB: He that troubleth his house, shall possess the winds: and he that is a fool shall serve the wise.

text.NRSV: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, but violence takes lives away.
text.Rotherham: The fruit of the righteous, is a tree of life, and, he that rescueth souls, is wise.
text.KJV: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise.
text.NABRE: The fruit of justice is a tree of life, and one who takes lives is a sage.
text.DRB: The fruit of the just man a tree of life: and he that gaineth souls, is wise.

text.NRSV: If the righteous are repaid on earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!
text.Rotherham: Lo! the righteous, in the earth shall be recompensed, how much more the lawless and the sinner.
text.KJV: Behold, the righteous shall be recompensed in the earth: much more the wicked and the sinner.
text.NABRE: If the just are recompensed on the earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!
text.DRB: If the just man receive in the earth, how much more the impious and sinner?

text.NRSV: Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but one who hates revelry is stupid.
text.Rotherham: He that loveth correction, loveth knowledge, but, he that hateth reproof, is brutish.
text.KJV: Whoso loveth instruction loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reproof is brutish.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but whoever hates reproof is stupid.
text.DRB: He that loveth discipline, loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reprehensions is unwise.

text.NRSV: The good obtain favor from the Lord, but he condemns those who devise evil.
text.Rotherham: A good man, obtaineth favour from Yahweh, but, a man of wicked devices, will he condemn.
text.KJV: A good man obtaineth favour of the Lord: but a man of wicked devices will he condemn.
text.NABRE: A good person wins favor from the Lord, but the schemer he condemns.
text.DRB: He that is good, shall draw grace from our Lord: but he that trusteth in his own cogitations, doth impiously.

text.NRSV: No one finds security by wickedness, but the root of the righteous will never be moved.
text.Rotherham: A son of earth, shall not be established, by lawlessness, but, the root of the righteous, shall not be disturbed.
text.KJV: A man shall not be established by wickedness: but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.
text.NABRE: No one is made secure by wickedness, but the root of the just will never be disturbed.
text.DRB: Man shall not be strengthened by impiety: and the root of the just shall not be moved.

text.NRSV: A good wife is the crown of her husband, but she who brings shame is like rottenness in his bones.
text.Rotherham: A virtuous woman, is the crown of her husband, but, a veritable decay in his bones, is she that causeth shame.
text.KJV: A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband: but she that maketh ashamed is as rottenness in his bones.
text.NABRE: A woman of worth is the crown of her husband, but a disgraceful one is like rot in his bones.
text.DRB: Adiligent woman is a crown to her husband: and putrefaction in his bones, she that doth things worthy of confusion.

text.NRSV: The thoughts of the righteous are just; the counsels of the wicked are treacherous.
text.Rotherham: The plans of the righteous, are just, but, the counsels of the lawless, are deceitful.
text.KJV: The thoughts of the righteous are right: but the counsels of the wicked are deceit.
text.NABRE: The plans of the just are right; the designs of the wicked are deceit.
text.DRB: The cogitations of the just are judgments: & the counsels of the impious are fraudulent.

text.NRSV: The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush, but the speech of the upright delivers them.
text.Rotherham: The words of the lawless, are—of lying in wait for blood, but, the mouth of the upright, shall deliver them.
text.KJV: The words of the wicked are to lie in wait for blood: but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.
text.NABRE: The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush, but the speech of the upright saves them.
text.DRB: The words of the impious lie in wait for blood: the mouth of the just shall deliver them.

text.NRSV: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, but the house of the righteous will stand.
text.Rotherham: Overthrown are the lawless and they are not, but, the house of the righteous, shall stand.
text.KJV: The wicked are overthrown, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand.
text.NABRE: Overthrow the wicked and they are no more, but the house of the just stands firm.
text.DRB: Turn the impious, and they shall not be: but the house of the just shall be permanent.

text.NRSV: One is commended for good sense, but a perverse mind is despised.
text.Rotherham: In proportion to his prudence, is a man, to be praised, but, the perverse in heart, shall be an object of contempt.
text.KJV: A man shall be commended according to his wisdom: but he that is of a perverse heart shall be despised.
text.NABRE: For their good sense people are praised, but the perverse of heart are despised.
text.DRB: Aman shall be known by his doctrine: but he that is vain and foolish, shall lie open to contempt.

text.NRSV: Better to be despised and have a servant, than to be self-important and have no food.
text.Rotherham: Better slighted, and have a servant, than to honour oneself, and come short of bread.
text.KJV: He that is despised, and hath a servant, is better than he that honoureth himself, and lacketh bread.
text.NABRE: Better to be slighted and have a servant than put on airs and lack bread.
text.DRB: Better is the poor and sufficient to himself, than he that is glorious and wanteth bread.

text.NRSV: The righteous know the needs of their animals, but the mercy of the wicked is cruel.
text.Rotherham: A righteous man, regardeth the desire of his beast, but, the compassions of the lawless, are cruel.
text.KJV: A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast: but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
text.NABRE: The just take care of their livestock, but the compassion of the wicked is cruel.
text.DRB: The just knoweth the lives of his beasts: but the bowels of the impious are cruel.

text.NRSV: Those who till their land will have plenty of food, but those who follow worthless pursuits have no sense.
text.Rotherham: He that tilleth his land, shall be filled with food, but, he that runneth after worthless men, lacketh sense.
text.KJV: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread: but he that followeth vain persons is void of understanding.
text.NABRE: Those who till their own land have food in plenty, but those who engage in idle pursuits lack sense.
text.DRB: He that tilleth his land, shall be filled with breads: but he that pursueth idleness is a very fool. He that is delighted in much quaffing of wine, leaveth contumely in his munitions.

text.NRSV: The wicked covet the proceeds of wickedness, but the root of the righteous bears fruit.
text.Rotherham: A lawless man craveth the prey of the wicked, but, the root of the righteous, is enduring.
text.KJV: The wicked desireth the net of evil men: but the root of the righteous yieldeth fruit.
text.NABRE: A wicked person desires the catch of evil people, but the root of the righteous will bear fruit.
text.DRB: The desire of the impious is the muniment of the most wicked: but the root of the just shall prosper.

text.NRSV: The wicked are ensnared by the transgression of their lips, but the righteous escape from trouble.
text.Rotherham: By the transgression of the lips, is the wicked man, ensnared, but the righteous, cometh out of distress.
text.KJV: The wicked is snared by the transgression of his lips: but the just shall come out of trouble.
text.NABRE: By the sin of their lips the wicked are ensnared, but the just escape from a tight spot.
text.DRB: For the sins of the lips' ruin approacheth to the evil man: but the just escapeth out of distress.

text.NRSV: From the fruit of the mouth one is filled with good things, and manual labor has its reward.
text.Rotherham: Of the fruit of a man’s mouth, shall he be satisfied with good, and, the dealing of the hands of a son of earth, shall be paid back to him.
text.KJV: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth: and the recompence of a man’s hands shall be rendered unto him.
text.NABRE: From the fruit of their mouths people have their fill of good, and the works of their hands come back upon them.
text.DRB: Of the fruit of his own mouth shall every man be replenished with good things, and according to the works of his hands it shall be repayed him.

text.NRSV: Fools think their own way is right, but the wise listen to advice.
