text.Rotherham: The way of the foolish, is right in his own eyes, but, he that hearkeneth to counsel, is wise.
text.KJV: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel is wise.
text.NABRE: The way of fools is right in their own eyes, but those who listen to advice are the wise.
text.DRB: The way of a fool is right in his eyes: but he that is wise heareth counsels.

text.NRSV: Fools show their anger at once, but the prudent ignore an insult.
text.Rotherham: A foolish man, on the [same] day, letteth his vexation be known, but, he that concealeth an affront, is prudent.
text.KJV: A fool’s wrath is presently known: but a prudent man covereth shame.
text.NABRE: Fools immediately show their anger, but the shrewd conceal contempt.
text.DRB: Afool by & by sheweth his anger: but he that dissembleth injuries is wise.

text.NRSV: Whoever speaks the truth gives honest evidence, but a false witness speaks deceitfully.
text.Rotherham: He that whispereth faithfulness, declareth righteousness, but, a false witness, is a fraud.
text.KJV: He that speaketh truth sheweth forth righteousness: but a false witness deceit.
text.NABRE: Whoever speaks honestly testifies truly, but the deceitful make lying witnesses.
text.DRB: He that speaketh that which he knoweth, is an utterer of justice: but he that lieth, is a fraudulent witness.

text.NRSV: Rash words are like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
text.Rotherham: There is who babbleth, as with thrusts of a sword, but, the tongue of the wise, hath healing.
text.KJV: There is that speaketh like the piercings of a sword: but the tongue of the wise is health.
text.NABRE: The babble of some people is like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise is healing.
text.DRB: There is that promiseth, and is pricked as it were with the sword of conscience: but the tongue of the wise is health.

text.NRSV: Truthful lips endure forever, but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
text.Rotherham: A lip that is faithful, shall be firm to futurity, but, only for a twinkling, is the tongue that is false.
text.KJV: The lip of truth shall be established for ever: but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
text.NABRE: Truthful lips endure forever, the lying tongue, for only a moment.
text.DRB: The lip of truth shall be stable for ever: but he that is an hasty witness, frameth a tongue of lying.

text.NRSV: Deceit is in the mind of those who plan evil, but those who counsel peace have joy.
text.Rotherham: Deceit, is in the heart of contrivers of mischief, but, to the counsellors of peace, shall be joy.
text.KJV: Deceit is in the heart of them that imagine evil: but to the counsellors of peace is joy.
text.NABRE: Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but those who counsel peace have joy.
text.DRB: Guile is in the heart of them that think evil things: but joy followeth them that give counsels of peace.

text.NRSV: No harm happens to the righteous, but the wicked are filled with trouble.
text.Rotherham: Unseemly to the righteous, is any trouble, but, the lawless, are full of misfortune.
text.KJV: There shall no evil happen to the just: but the wicked shall be filled with mischief.
text.NABRE: No harm befalls the just, but the wicked are overwhelmed with misfortune.
text.DRB: It shall not make the just sorry what soever shall fall to him: but the impious shall be replenished with evil.

text.NRSV: Lying lips are an abomination to the Lord, but those who act faithfully are his delight.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, are lips that are false, but, he that dealeth faithfully, is his delight.
text.KJV: Lying lips are abomination to the Lord: but they that deal truly are his delight.
text.NABRE: Lying lips are an abomination to the Lord, but those who are truthful, his delight.
text.DRB: Lying lips are an abomination to our Lord: but they that do faithfully please him.

text.NRSV: One who is clever conceals knowledge, but the mind of a fool broadcasts folly.
text.Rotherham: A prudent man, concealeth knowledge, but, the heart of dullards, proclaimeth folly.
text.KJV: A prudent man concealeth knowledge: but the heart of fools proclaimeth foolishness.
text.NABRE: The shrewd conceal knowledge, but the hearts of fools proclaim folly.
text.DRB: Acircumspect man concealeth knowledge: and the heart of the unwise provoketh folly.

text.NRSV: The hand of the diligent will rule, while the lazy will be put to forced labor.
text.Rotherham: The hand of the diligent, shall bear rule, but, the indolent, shall come under tribute.
text.KJV: The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.
text.NABRE: The diligent hand will govern, but sloth makes for forced labor.
text.DRB: The hand of the strong shall rule, but that which is slothful, shall serve under tributes.

text.NRSV: Anxiety weighs down the human heart, but a good word cheers it up.
text.Rotherham: When anxiety is in the heart of a man, one may bow him down, but, a good word, maketh him glad.
text.KJV: Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad.
text.NABRE: Worry weighs down the heart, but a kind word gives it joy.
text.DRB: Pensiveness in the heart of a man shall humble him, & with a good word he shall be made glad.

text.NRSV: The righteous at best are better than their neighbors, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
text.Rotherham: The righteous man guideth his neighbour, but, the way of the lawless, leadeth them astray.
text.KJV: The righteous is more excellent than his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them.
text.NABRE: The just act as guides to their neighbors, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
text.DRB: He that neglecteth damage for a friend, is just: but the way of the impious shall deceive them.

text.NRSV: The lazy do not roast their game, but the diligent possess precious wealth.
text.Rotherham: Indolence, roasteth not, his own game, but, the substance of a man, is precious, when he is diligent.
text.KJV: The slothful man roasteth not that which he took in hunting: but the substance of a diligent man is precious.
text.NABRE: Sloth does not catch its prey, but the wealth of the diligent is splendid.
text.DRB: The fraudulent man shall not find gain: and the substance of a man shall be the price of gold.

text.NRSV: In the path of righteousness there is life, in her tread there is no death.
text.Rotherham: In the way of righteousness, is life, and, in the path thereof, immortality,
text.KJV: In the way of righteousness is life; and in the pathway thereof there is no death.
text.NABRE: In the path of justice is life, but the way of abomination leads to death.
text.DRB: In the path of justice, life: but the by-way leadeth to death.

text.NRSV: A wise child loves discipline, but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
text.Rotherham: A wise son, [cometh of] a father’s correction, but, a scoffer, heareth not a rebuke.
text.KJV: A wise son heareth his father’s instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 A wise son loves correction, but the scoffer heeds no rebuke.
text.DRB: Awise son is the doctrine of the father: but he that is a scorner, heareth not when he is rebuked.

text.NRSV: From the fruit of their words good people eat good things, but the desire of the treacherous is for violence.
text.Rotherham: Of the fruit of his mouth, shall a man eat what is good, but, the soul of the treacherous, [shall be sated with] violence.
text.KJV: A man shall eat good by the fruit of his mouth: but the soul of the transgressors shall eat violence.
text.NABRE: From the fruit of the mouth one enjoys good things, but from the throat of the treacherous comes violence.
text.DRB: Of the fruit of his own mouth man shall be filled with good things: but the soul of the prevaricators is wicked.

text.NRSV: Those who guard their mouths preserve their lives; those who open wide their lips come to ruin.
text.Rotherham: He that watcheth his mouth, guardeth his soul, He that openeth wide his lips, [it shall be] his ruin.
text.KJV: He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction.
text.NABRE: Those who guard their mouths preserve themselves; those who open wide their lips bring ruin.
text.DRB: He that keepeth his mouth, keepeth his soul: but he that is unadvised to speak shall feel evils.

text.NRSV: The appetite of the lazy craves, and gets nothing, while the appetite of the diligent is richly supplied.
text.Rotherham: The sluggard, desireth, but his soul hath, nothing. But, the soul of the diligent, shall be enriched.
text.KJV: The soul of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.
text.NABRE: The appetite of the sluggard craves but has nothing, but the appetite of the diligent is amply satisfied.
text.DRB: The sluggard will and will not: but the soul of them that work, shall be made fat.

text.NRSV: The righteous hate falsehood, but the wicked act shamefully and disgracefully.
text.Rotherham: A word of falsehood, the righteous man, hateth, but, the lawless, causeth shame and disgrace.
text.KJV: A righteous man hateth lying: but a wicked man is loathsome, and cometh to shame.
text.NABRE: The just hate deceitful words, but the wicked are odious and disgraceful.
text.DRB: The just shall detest a lying word: but the impious confoundeth, and shall be confounded.

text.NRSV: Righteousness guards one whose way is upright, but sin overthrows the wicked.
text.Rotherham: Righteousness, guardeth the man of blameless way, but, lawlessness, overthroweth the sinner.
text.KJV: Righteousness keepeth him that is upright in the way: but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.
text.NABRE: Justice guards one who walks honestly, but sin leads the wicked astray.
text.DRB: Justice keepeth the way of the innocent: but impiety supplanteth the sinner.

text.NRSV: Some pretend to be rich, yet have nothing; others pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.
text.Rotherham: There is who feigneth himself rich, yet hath nothing at all, who pleadeth poverty, yet hath great substance.
text.KJV: There is that maketh himself rich, yet hath nothing: there is that maketh himself poor, yet hath great riches.
text.NABRE: One acts rich but has nothing; another acts poor but has great wealth.
text.DRB: There is one as it were with riches whereas he hath nothing: and there is as it were poor, whereas he is in much riches.

text.NRSV: Wealth is a ransom for a person’s life, but the poor get no threats.
text.Rotherham: The ransom of a man’s life, is his wealth, but, the poor, heareth not rebuke.
text.KJV: The ransom of a man’s life are his riches: but the poor heareth not rebuke.
text.NABRE: People’s riches serve as ransom for their lives, but the poor do not even hear a threat.
text.DRB: The redemption of a man's life, his riches: but he that is poor, beareth not reprehension.

text.NRSV: The light of the righteous rejoices, but the lamp of the wicked goes out.
text.Rotherham: The light of the righteous, rejoiceth, but, the lamp of the lawless, goeth out.
text.KJV: The light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.
text.NABRE: The light of the just gives joy, but the lamp of the wicked goes out.
text.DRB: The light of the just maketh glad: but the candle of the impious shall be extinguished.

text.NRSV: By insolence the heedless make strife, but wisdom is with those who take advice.
text.Rotherham: Only by pride, doth one cause contention, but, with the well-advised, is wisdom.
text.KJV: Only by pride cometh contention: but with the well advised is wisdom.
text.NABRE: The stupid sow discord by their insolence, but wisdom is with those who take counsel.
text.DRB: Among the proud there are always brawls: but they that do all things with counsel, are ruled by wisdom.

text.NRSV: Wealth hastily gotten will dwindle, but those who gather little by little will increase it.
text.Rotherham: Wealth gotten by greed, diminisheth, but, he that gathereth by little, increaseth.
text.KJV: Wealth gotten by vanity shall be diminished: but he that gathereth by labour shall increase.
text.NABRE: Wealth won quickly dwindles away, but gathered little by little, it grows.
text.DRB: Substance hastened shall be diminished: but that which by little and little is gathered with the hand, shall be multiplied.

text.NRSV: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
text.Rotherham: Hope deferred, sickeneth the heart,—but, a tree of life, is desire fulfilled.
text.KJV: Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life.
text.NABRE: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a wish fulfilled is a tree of life.
text.DRB: Hope that is deferred afflicteth the soul: a tree of life the desire of ckoning.

text.NRSV: Those who despise the word bring destruction on themselves, but those who respect the commandment will be rewarded.
text.Rotherham: He that despiseth a matter, shall get pledged thereto, but, he that revereth a commandment, the same shall be recompensed.
text.KJV: Whoso despiseth the word shall be destroyed: but he that feareth the commandment shall be rewarded.
text.NABRE: Whoever despises the word must pay for it, but whoever reveres the command will be rewarded.
text.DRB: Who so detracteth from any thing, he bindeth himself for the time to come: but he that feareth the precept, shall converse in peace. Guileful souls err in sins: the just are merciful & have pity.

text.NRSV: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, so that one may avoid the snares of death.
text.Rotherham: the instruction of the wise, is a well-spring of life, by departing from the snares of death.
text.KJV: The law of the wise is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.
text.NABRE: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, turning one from the snares of death.
text.DRB: The law of a wise man a fountain of life, that he may decline from the ruin of death.

text.NRSV: Good sense wins favor, but the way of the faithless is their ruin.
text.Rotherham: Sound discretion, yieldeth favour, but, the way of the treacherous, is rugged.
text.KJV: Good understanding giveth favour: but the way of transgressors is hard.
text.NABRE: Good sense brings favor, but the way of the faithless is their ruin.
text.DRB: Good doctrine shall give grace: in the way of contemners a whirlpool.

text.NRSV: The clever do everything with knowledge, but fools display their folly.
text.Rotherham: Every prudent man, maketh use of knowledge, but, a dullard, spreadeth folly.
text.KJV: Every prudent man dealeth with knowledge: but a fool layeth open his folly.
text.NABRE: The shrewd always act prudently but the foolish parade folly.
text.DRB: The subtle man doth all things with counsel: but he that is a fool openeth folly.

text.NRSV: A bad messenger overthrows the sender, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
text.Rotherham: A lawless messenger, falleth into mischief, but, a faithful herald, bringeth healing.
text.KJV: A wicked messenger falleth into mischief: but a faithful ambassador is health.
text.NABRE: A wicked messenger brings on disaster, but a trustworthy envoy is a healing remedy.
text.DRB: The messenger of the impious shall fall into evil: but a faithful legate, is health.

text.NRSV: Poverty and disgrace are for the one who ignores instruction, but one who heeds reproof is honored.
text.Rotherham: Poverty and contempt, are for him that neglecteth correction, but, he that regardeth reproof, shall be honoured.
text.KJV: Poverty and shame shall be to him that refuseth instruction: but he that regardeth reproof shall be honoured.
text.NABRE: Poverty and shame befall those who let go of discipline, but those who hold on to reproof receive honor.
text.DRB: Poverty, and ignominy, to him that forsaketh discipline: but he that yieldeth to him that rebuketh, shall be glorified.

text.NRSV: A desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but to turn away from evil is an abomination to fools.
text.Rotherham: A desire fulfilled, is sweet to the soul, but it is, an abomination to the lawless, to depart from evil.
text.KJV: The desire accomplished is sweet to the soul: but it is abomination to fools to depart from evil.
text.NABRE: Desire fulfilled delights the soul, but turning from evil is an abomination to fools.
text.DRB: Desire if it be accomplished, delighteth the soul: fools detest them that fly evil things.

text.NRSV: Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise, but the companion of fools suffers harm.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh with the wise, becometh wise, but, the friend of dullards, becometh foolish.
text.KJV: He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed.
text.NABRE: Walk with the wise and you become wise, but the companion of fools fares badly.
text.DRB: He that walketh with the wise, shall be wise: a friend of fools shall be made like.

text.NRSV: Misfortune pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.
text.Rotherham: Evil pursueth, sinners, but, unto the righteous, shall good be recompensed.
text.KJV: Evil pursueth sinners: but to the righteous good shall be repayed.
text.NABRE: Misfortune pursues sinners, but the just shall be recompensed with good.
text.DRB: Evil pursueth sinners, and to the just good things shall be repayed.

text.NRSV: The good leave an inheritance to their children’s children, but the sinner’s wealth is laid up for the righteous.
text.Rotherham: A good man, leaveth an inheritance to children’s children, but, laid up for the righteous, is the wealth of the sinner.
text.KJV: A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children’s children: and the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the just.
text.NABRE: The good leave an inheritance to their children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored up for the just.
text.DRB: The good man leaveth heirs, sons, and nephews: and the substance of the sinner is kept for the just.

text.NRSV: The field of the poor may yield much food, but it is swept away through injustice.
text.Rotherham: Much food, is in the fallow ground of the poor, but there is that is swept away, for want of justice.
text.KJV: Much food is in the tillage of the poor: but there is that is destroyed for want of judgment.
text.NABRE: The tillage of the poor yields abundant food, but possessions are swept away for lack of justice.
text.DRB: Much meat in the tilled grounds of the fathers: and to others they are gathered without judgment.

text.NRSV: Those who spare the rod hate their children, but those who love them are diligent to discipline them.
text.Rotherham: He that withholdeth his rod, hateth his son,—but, he that loveth him, carefully correcteth him.
text.KJV: He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.
text.NABRE: Whoever spares the rod hates the child, but whoever loves will apply discipline.
text.DRB: He that spareth the rod, hateth his child: but he that loveth him doth instantly nurture him.

text.NRSV: The righteous have enough to satisfy their appetite, but the belly of the wicked is empty.
text.Rotherham: The righteous, eateth to satisfy his appetite, but, the belly of the lawless, shall want.
text.KJV: The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul: but the belly of the wicked shall want.
text.NABRE: When the just eat, their hunger is appeased; but the belly of the wicked suffers want.
text.DRB: The just eateth and filleth his soul: but the belly of the impious unsatiable.

text.NRSV: The wise woman builds her house, but the foolish tears it down with her own hands.
text.Rotherham: Every, wise woman, buildeth up her house, but, a foolish one, with her own hands, would break it down.
text.KJV: Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Wisdom builds her house, but Folly tears hers down with her own hands.
text.DRB: Awise woman buildeth her house: the unwise will with her hands destroy that also which is built.

text.NRSV: Those who walk uprightly fear the Lord, but one who is devious in conduct despises him.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh in his uprightness, is one who revereth Yahweh, but, he that is crooked in his way, is one who despiseth him.
text.KJV: He that walketh in his uprightness feareth the Lord: but he that is perverse in his ways despiseth him.
text.NABRE: Those who walk uprightly fear the Lord, but those who are devious in their ways spurn him.
text.DRB: He that walketh in the right way, & feareth God, is despised of him, that goeth an infamous way.

text.NRSV: The talk of fools is a rod for their backs, but the lips of the wise preserve them.
text.Rotherham: In the mouth of the foolish, is a haughty rod, but, as for the lips of the wise, thou shalt give heed to them!
text.KJV: In the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride: but the lips of the wise shall preserve them.
text.NABRE: In the mouth of the fool is a rod for pride, but the lips of the wise preserve them.
text.DRB: In the mouth of a fool the rod of pride: but the lips of the wise keep them.

text.NRSV: Where there are no oxen, the manger is clean; but abundant crops come by the strength of the ox.
text.Rotherham: Without cattle, the crib is clean, but, much increase, is in the strength of the ox.
text.KJV: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean: but much increase is by the strength of the ox.
text.NABRE: Where there are no oxen, the crib is clean; but abundant crops come through the strength of the bull.
text.DRB: Where oxen are not, the stall is empty: but where much corn is, there is the ox's strength manifest.

text.NRSV: A faithful witness does not lie, but a false witness breathes out lies.
text.Rotherham: A faithful witness, will not deceive, but a false witness, uttereth deceitful things.
text.KJV: A faithful witness will not lie: but a false witness will utter lies.
text.NABRE: A trustworthy witness does not lie, but one who spouts lies makes a lying witness.
text.DRB: Afaithful witness will not lie: but a deceitful witness uttereth a lie.

text.NRSV: A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain, but knowledge is easy for one who has understanding.
text.Rotherham: A scoffer seeketh wisdom, and there is none, but, knowledge, to the discerning, is easy.
text.KJV: A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth.
text.NABRE: The scoffer seeks wisdom in vain, but knowledge is easy for the intelligent.
text.DRB: Ascorner seeketh wisdom and findeth it not: the doctrine of the prudent is easy.

text.NRSV: Leave the presence of a fool, for there you do not find words of knowledge.
text.Rotherham: Get thee from the presence of a man that is a dullard, when thou perceivest not the lips of knowledge.
text.KJV: Go from the presence of a foolish man, when thou perceivest not in him the lips of knowledge.
text.NABRE: Go from the face of the fool; you get no knowledge from such lips.
text.DRB: Go against a foolish man, and he knoweth not the lips of prudence.

text.NRSV: The wisdom of the clever is to understand their way, but the folly of fools is deceiving.
text.Rotherham: The wisdom of the prudent, is to understand his way, but, the folly of dullards, is a fraud.
text.KJV: The wisdom of the prudent is to understand his way: but the folly of fools is deceit.
text.NABRE: The wisdom of the shrewd enlightens their way, but the folly of fools is deceit.
text.DRB: The wisdom of a discreet man is to understand his way: and the imprudence of fools erreth.

text.NRSV: Fools mock at the guilt offering, but the upright enjoy God’s favor.
text.Rotherham: the foolish, scoff at guilt, but, between the upright, is good pleasure.
text.KJV: Fools make a mock at sin: but among the righteous there is favour.
text.NABRE: The wicked scorn a guilt offering, but the upright find acceptance.
text.DRB: Afool will laugh at sin, & among the just grace shall abide.

text.NRSV: The heart knows its own bitterness, and no stranger shares its joy.
text.Rotherham: The heart, knoweth its own bitterness, and, in its joy, no stranger shareth.
text.KJV: The heart knoweth his own bitterness; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy.
text.NABRE: The heart knows its own bitterness, and its joy no stranger shares.
text.DRB: The heart that knoweth the bitterness of his soul, in his joy shall not the stranger be mingled.

text.NRSV: The house of the wicked is destroyed, but the tent of the upright flourishes.
text.Rotherham: The house of the lawless, shall be destroyed, but, the tent of the upright, shall flourish.
text.KJV: The house of the wicked shall be overthrown: but the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish.
text.NABRE: The house of the wicked will be destroyed, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
text.DRB: The house of the impious shall be rased: the tabernacles of the just shall spring.

text.NRSV: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way to death.
text.Rotherham: There is a way that enticeth a man, but, at the latter end thereof, are the ways of death!
text.KJV: There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.
text.NABRE: Sometimes a way seems right, but the end of it leads to death!
text.DRB: There is a way, which seemeth to a man just: but the later ends thereof lead to death.

text.NRSV: Even in laughter the heart is sad, and the end of joy is grief.
text.Rotherham: Even, in laughter, the heart may be in pain, and, the latter end of gladness, be grief.
text.KJV: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth is heaviness.
text.NABRE: Even in laughter the heart may be sad, and the end of joy may be sorrow.
text.DRB: Laughter shall be mingled with sorrow, and mourning occupieth the later ends of joy.

text.NRSV: The perverse get what their ways deserve, and the good, what their deeds deserve.
text.Rotherham: With his own ways, shall be filled the backslider in heart, and, from himself, shall a good man [be satisfied].
text.KJV: The backslider in heart shall be filled with his own ways: and a good man shall be satisfied from himself.
text.NABRE: From their own ways turncoats are sated, from their own actions, the loyal.
text.DRB: Afool shall be replenished with his ways, and the good man shall be above him.

text.NRSV: The simple believe everything, but the clever consider their steps.
text.Rotherham: The simple, believeth every word, but, the prudent, looketh well to his going.
text.KJV: The simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.
text.NABRE: The naive believe everything, but the shrewd watch their steps.
text.DRB: The innocent believeth every word: the discreet man considereth his steps.

text.NRSV: The wise are cautious and turn away from evil, but fools throw off restraint and are careless.
text.Rotherham: A wise man, feareth, and avoideth evil, but, a dullard, is haughty and confident.
text.KJV: A wise man feareth, and departeth from evil: but the fool rageth, and is confident.
text.NABRE: The wise person is cautious and turns from evil; the fool is reckless and gets embroiled.
text.DRB: Awise man feareth and declineth from evil: the fool leapeth over and is confident.

text.NRSV: Those who are quick-tempered help folly, and those who scheme are hated.
text.Rotherham: The impatient, worketh folly, and, a man of wicked devices, is hated.
text.KJV: He that is soon angry dealeth foolishly: and a man of wicked devices is hated.
text.NABRE: The quick-tempered make fools of themselves, and schemers are hated.
text.DRB: The impatient man shall work folly: and the subtle man is odious.

text.NRSV: The simple are adorned with folly, but the clever are crowned with knowledge.
text.Rotherham: The simple inherit folly, but, the shrewd, crowneth himself with knowledge.
text.KJV: The simple inherit folly: but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
text.NABRE: The simple have folly as an adornment, but the shrewd wear knowledge as a crown.
text.DRB: The childish man shall possess folly, and the prudent shall expect knowledge.

text.NRSV: The evil bow down before the good, the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: Abased are the wicked, before the good, and, the lawless, at the gates of the righteous.
text.KJV: The evil bow before the good; and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
text.NABRE: The malicious bow down before the good, and the wicked, at the gates of the just.
text.DRB: The evil shall lie down before the good, and the impious before the gates of the just.

text.NRSV: The poor are disliked even by their neighbors, but the rich have many friends.
text.Rotherham: Even by his neighbour, is a poor man hated, but, the lovers of the rich man, are many.
text.KJV: The poor is hated even of his own neighbour: but the rich hath many friends.
text.NABRE: Even by their neighbors the poor are despised, but a rich person’s friends are many.
text.DRB: The poor shall be odious even to his neighbour: but the friends of the rich be many.

text.NRSV: Those who despise their neighbors are sinners, but happy are those who are kind to the poor.
text.Rotherham: He that sheweth contempt for his neighbour, sinneth, but, he that sheweth favour to the afflicted, how happy is he!
text.KJV: He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.
text.NABRE: Whoever despises the hungry comes up short, but happy the one who is kind to the poor!
text.DRB: He that despiseth his neighbour, sinneth: but he that hath pity on the poor, shall be blessed.

text.NRSV: Do they not err who plan evil? Those who plan good find loyalty and faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: Shall they not, become vagrants, who devise evil? but, lovingkindness and faithfulness, [be to] them who devise good.
text.KJV: Do they not err that devise evil? but mercy and truth shall be to them that devise good.
text.NABRE: Do not those who plan evil go astray? But those who plan good win steadfast loyalty.
text.DRB: They err that work evil: mercy and truth prepare good things.

text.NRSV: In all toil there is profit, but mere talk leads only to poverty.
text.Rotherham: By all labour, there will be abundance, but, the talk of the lips, tendeth only to want.
text.KJV: In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury.
text.NABRE: In all labor there is profit, but mere talk tends only to loss.
text.DRB: In every work there shall be aboundance: but where many words are, there is oftentimes poverty.

text.NRSV: The crown of the wise is their wisdom, but folly is the garland of fools.
text.Rotherham: the crown of the wise, is their wealth, but, the folly of dullards, is, folly.
text.KJV: The crown of the wise is their riches: but the foolishness of fools is folly.
text.NABRE: The crown of the wise is wealth; the diadem of fools is folly.
text.DRB: The crown of the wise, their riches: the folly of fools, imprudence.

text.NRSV: A truthful witness saves lives, but one who utters lies is a betrayer.
text.Rotherham: A deliverer of souls, is a faithful witness, but, he that uttereth falsehoods, is a fraud.
text.KJV: A true witness delivereth souls: but a deceitful witness speaketh lies.
text.NABRE: The truthful witness saves lives, but whoever utters lies is a betrayer.
text.DRB: Afaithful witness delivereth souls: and the dissembler uttereth lies.

text.NRSV: In the fear of the Lord one has strong confidence, and one’s children will have a refuge.
text.Rotherham: In the reverence of Yahweh, is strong security, and, his children, shall have a place of refuge.
text.KJV: In the fear of the Lord is strong confidence: and his children shall have a place of refuge.
text.NABRE: The fear of the Lord is a strong defense, a refuge even for one’s children.
text.DRB: In the fear of our Lord is confidence of strength, and to his children there shall be hope.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, so that one may avoid the snares of death.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is a well-spring of life, that a man may avoid the snares of death.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.
text.NABRE: The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, turning one from the snares of death.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord a fountain of life, that he may decline from the ruin of death.

text.NRSV: The glory of a king is a multitude of people; without people a prince is ruined.
text.Rotherham: In the multitude of a people, is the majesty of the king, but, in the ceasing of population, is the ruin of the prince.
text.KJV: In the multitude of people is the king’s honour: but in the want of people is the destruction of the prince.
text.NABRE: A multitude of subjects is the glory of the king; but if his people are few, a prince is ruined.
text.DRB: In the multitude of people the dignity of the king: and in fewness of people the ignominy of the Prince.

text.NRSV: Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but one who has a hasty temper exalts folly.
text.Rotherham: One slow to anger, hath great understanding, but, the hasty in spirit, exalteth folly.
text.KJV: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding: but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly.
text.NABRE: Long-suffering results in great wisdom; a short temper raises folly high.
text.DRB: He that is patient, is governed with much wisdom: but he that is impatient, exalteth his folly.

text.NRSV: A tranquil mind gives life to the flesh, but passion makes the bones rot.
text.Rotherham: The life of the whole body, is a tranquil mind, but, a decay of the bones, is jealousy.
text.KJV: A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones.
text.NABRE: A tranquil mind gives life to the body, but jealousy rots the bones.
text.DRB: Health of heart, the life of the flesh: envy, the putrefaction of the bones.

text.NRSV: Those who oppress the poor insult their Maker, but those who are kind to the needy honor him.
text.Rotherham: The oppressor of the poor, hath reproached his Maker, but he that sheweth favour to the needy, is one who, holdeth Him in honour.
text.KJV: He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker: but he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor.
text.NABRE: Those who oppress the poor revile their Maker, but those who are kind to the needy honor him.
text.DRB: He that doth calumniate the needy, upbraideth his maker: but he honoureth him, that hath pity on the poor.

text.NRSV: The wicked are overthrown by their evildoing, but the righteous find a refuge in their integrity.
text.Rotherham: By his own wickedness, shall the lawless man, be thrust down, but the righteous, seeketh refuge in his integrity.
text.KJV: The wicked is driven away in his wickedness: but the righteous hath hope in his death.
text.NABRE: The wicked are overthrown by their wickedness, but the just find a refuge in their integrity.
text.DRB: The impious shall be expelled in his malice: but the just hopeth in his death.

text.NRSV: Wisdom settles in the mind of one who has understanding, but it is not known in the heart of fools.
text.Rotherham: In the heart of the intelligent, reposeth wisdom, but, in the midst of dullards, it maketh itself known.
text.KJV: Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known.
text.NABRE: Wisdom can remain silent in the discerning heart, but among fools she must make herself known.
text.DRB: In the heart of the prudent resteth wisdom, & it shall instruct all the unlearned.

text.NRSV: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to any people.
text.Rotherham: Righteousness, exalteth a nation, but, a reproach to any people, is sin.
text.KJV: Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people.
text.NABRE: Justice exalts a nation, but sin is a people’s disgrace.
text.DRB: Justice advanceth a nation: but sin maketh peoples miserable.

text.NRSV: A king favors a servant who acts wisely, but his anger falls on one who acts shamefully.
text.Rotherham: The good-pleasure of a king, is due to a servant who is discreet, but, his indignation, shall be against him that bringeth shame.
text.KJV: The king’s favour is toward a wise servant: but his wrath is against him that causeth shame.
text.NABRE: The king favors the skillful servant, but the shameless one incurs his wrath.
text.DRB: Aservant that understandeth is acceptable to the king: he that is unprofitable shall sustain his anger.

text.NRSV: A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
text.Rotherham: A soft answer, turneth away wrath, but a cutting word raiseth anger.
text.KJV: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 A mild answer turns back wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
text.DRB: Asoft answer breaketh anger: and a hard word raiseth up fury.

text.NRSV: The tongue of the wise dispenses knowledge, but the mouths of fools pour out folly.
text.Rotherham: The tongue of the wise, adorneth knowledge,—but, the mouth of dullards, belcheth out folly.
text.KJV: The tongue of the wise useth knowledge aright: but the mouth of fools poureth out foolishness.
text.NABRE: The tongue of the wise pours out knowledge, but the mouth of fools spews folly.
text.DRB: The tongue of the wise adorneth knowledge: the mouth of fools boileth forth folly.

text.NRSV: The eyes of the Lord are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good.
text.Rotherham: In every place, are the eyes of Yahweh, observing the evil and the good.
text.KJV: The eyes of the Lord are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.
text.NABRE: The eyes of the Lord are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good.
text.DRB: In every place the eyes of our Lord behold the good and the evil.

text.NRSV: A gentle tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it breaks the spirit.
text.Rotherham: Gentleness of the tongue, is a tree of life, but, crookedness therein, a grievous wound in the spirit.
text.KJV: A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit.
text.NABRE: A soothing tongue is a tree of life, but a perverse one breaks the spirit.
text.DRB: Apeaceable tongue is a tree of life: but that which is immoderate, shall break the spirit.

text.NRSV: A fool despises a father’s instruction, but the one who heeds admonition is prudent.
text.Rotherham: A foolish [son], spurneth the correction of his father, but, he that heedeth a reproof, sheweth prudence.
text.KJV: A fool despiseth his father’s instruction: but he that regardeth reproof is prudent.
text.NABRE: The fool spurns a father’s instruction, but whoever heeds reproof is prudent.
text.DRB: Afool scorneth the discipline of his father: but he that regardeth reprehensions, shall become more prudent. In abundant justice there is greater force: but the cogitations of the impious shall be rooted out.

text.NRSV: In the house of the righteous there is much treasure, but trouble befalls the income of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: The household of the righteous man, is a great treasure, but, in the increase of the lawless, is disturbance.
text.KJV: In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble.
text.NABRE: In the house of the just there are ample resources, but the harvest of the wicked is in peril.
text.DRB: The house of the just is very much strength: and in the fruits of the impious is perturbation.

text.NRSV: The lips of the wise spread knowledge; not so the minds of fools.
text.Rotherham: The lips of the wise, scatter abroad knowledge, but, the heart of dullards, is not right.
text.KJV: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish doeth not so.
text.NABRE: The lips of the wise spread knowledge, but the heart of fools is not steadfast.
text.DRB: The lips of the wise shall sow knowledge: the heart of fools shall be unlike.

text.NRSV: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
text.Rotherham: The sacrifice of lawless men, is an abomination to Yahweh, but, the prayer of the upright, is his delight.
text.KJV: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord: but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
text.NABRE: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
text.DRB: The victims of the impious are abominable to our Lord: the vows of the just are acceptable.

text.NRSV: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, but he loves the one who pursues righteousness.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, is the way of the lawless, but, the pursuer of righteousness, he loveth.
text.KJV: The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
text.NABRE: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, but he loves one who pursues justice.
text.DRB: The way of the impious is abomination to our Lord: he that followeth justice is beloved of him.

text.NRSV: There is severe discipline for one who forsakes the way, but one who hates a rebuke will die.
text.Rotherham: There is, grievous correction, for him that forsaketh the right path, He that hateth rebuke, shall die.
text.KJV: Correction is grievous unto him that forsaketh the way: and he that hateth reproof shall die.
text.NABRE: Discipline seems bad to those going astray; one who hates reproof will die.
text.DRB: The doctrine is evil of them that forsake the way of life: he that hateth reprehensions shall die.

text.NRSV: Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the Lord; how much more human hearts!
text.Rotherham: Hades and destruction, are before Yahweh, how much more then, the hearts of the sons of men.
text.KJV: Hell and destruction are before the Lord: how much more then the hearts of the children of men?
text.NABRE: Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the Lord; how much more the hearts of mortals!
text.DRB: Hell and perdition are before our Lord: how much more the hearts of the children of men?

text.NRSV: Scoffers do not like to be rebuked; they will not go to the wise.
text.Rotherham: A scoffer, loveth not, one who reproveth him, unto the wise, doth he not go.
text.KJV: A scorner loveth not one that reproveth him: neither will he go unto the wise.
text.NABRE: Scoffers do not love reproof; to the wise they will not go.
text.DRB: The pestilent man loveth not him that rebuketh him: nor goeth to the wise.

text.NRSV: A glad heart makes a cheerful countenance, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is broken.
text.Rotherham: A joyful heart, maketh a pleasing countenance,—but, in sorrow of heart, is a stricken spirit.
text.KJV: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance: but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken.
text.NABRE: A glad heart lights up the face, but an anguished heart breaks the spirit.
text.DRB: Aglad heart cheereth the face: in pensiveness of mind the spirit is cast down.

text.NRSV: The mind of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.
text.Rotherham: The heart of the intelligent, seeketh knowledge, but, the mouth of dullards, feedeth on folly.
text.KJV: The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness.
text.NABRE: The discerning heart seeks knowledge, but the mouth of fools feeds on folly.
text.DRB: The heart of the wise seeketh doctrine: and the mouth of fools is fed with unskillfulness.

text.NRSV: All the days of the poor are hard, but a cheerful heart has a continual feast.
text.Rotherham: All the days of the afflicted, are sorrowful, but, a cheerful heart, is a continual banquet.
text.KJV: All the days of the afflicted are evil: but he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast.
text.NABRE: All the days of the poor are evil, but a good heart is a continual feast.
text.DRB: All the days of the poor are evil: a secure mind is as it were a continual feast.

text.NRSV: Better is a little with the fear of the Lord than great treasure and trouble with it.
text.Rotherham: Better is a little, with the reverence of Yahweh, than great treasure, and disquietude therewith.
text.KJV: Better is little with the fear of the Lord than great treasure and trouble therewith.
text.NABRE: Better a little with fear of the Lord than a great fortune with anxiety.
text.DRB: Better is a little with the fear of our Lord, than great treasures and unsatiable.

text.NRSV: Better is a dinner of vegetables where love is than a fatted ox and hatred with it.
text.Rotherham: Better is an allowance of herbs, and love, there, than a fatted ox, and hatred therewith.
text.KJV: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is, than a stalled ox and hatred therewith.
text.NABRE: Better a dish of herbs where love is than a fatted ox and hatred with it.
text.DRB: It is better to be called to herbs with charity: than to a fatted calf with hatred.

text.NRSV: Those who are hot-tempered stir up strife, but those who are slow to anger calm contention.
text.Rotherham: A wrathful man, stirreth up strife, but, he that is slow to anger, calmeth contention.
text.KJV: A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.
text.NABRE: The ill-tempered stir up strife, but the patient settle disputes.
text.DRB: An angry man stirreth brawls: he that is patient appeaseth those that are raised.

text.NRSV: The way of the lazy is overgrown with thorns, but the path of the upright is a level highway.
text.Rotherham: The way of the sluggard, is like a thorn hedge, but, the path of the upright, is a raised road.
text.KJV: The way of the slothful man is as an hedge of thorns: but the way of the righteous is made plain.
text.NABRE: The way of the sluggard is like a thorn hedge, but the path of the diligent is a highway.
text.DRB: The way of the slothful is as an hedge of thorns: the way of the just is without offence.

text.NRSV: A wise child makes a glad father, but the foolish despise their mothers.
text.Rotherham: A wise son, maketh a glad father,—but, a dullard, despiseth his own mother.
text.KJV: A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.
text.NABRE: A wise son gives his father joy, but a fool despises his mother.
text.DRB: Awise son maketh the father joyful: and the foolish man despiseth his mother.

text.NRSV: Folly is a joy to one who has no sense, but a person of understanding walks a straight course.
text.Rotherham: Folly, is joy to him that lacketh sense,—but, a man of understanding, taketh a straight course.
text.KJV: Folly is joy to him that is destitute of wisdom: but a man of understanding walketh uprightly.
text.NABRE: Folly is joy to the senseless, but the person of understanding goes the straight way.
text.DRB: Folly is joy to a fool: and the wise man directeth his steps.

text.NRSV: Without counsel, plans go wrong, but with many advisers they succeed.
text.Rotherham: Plans, are frustrated, for lack of consultation, but, by the multitude of counsellors, shall counsel, be established.
text.KJV: Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.
text.NABRE: Plans fail when there is no counsel, but they succeed when advisers are many.
text.DRB: Cogitations are dissipated where there is no counsel: but where many counsellers are, they are confirmed.

text.NRSV: To make an apt answer is a joy to anyone, and a word in season, how good it is!
text.Rotherham: A man hath joy, in the answer of his own mouth, and, a word in its season,—how good!
text.KJV: A man hath joy by the answer of his mouth: and a word spoken in due season, how good is it!
text.NABRE: One has joy from an apt response; a word in season, how good it is!
text.DRB: Aman rejoiceth in the sentence of his mouth: and a word in due time is best.

text.NRSV: For the wise the path of life leads upward, so that they may avoid Sheol below.
text.Rotherham: The way of life, is upwards to the prudent, that he may depart from hades beneath.
text.KJV: The way of life is above to the wise, that he may depart from hell beneath.
text.NABRE: The path of life leads upward for the prudent, turning them from Sheol below.
text.DRB: The path of life above the learned, that he may decline from the lowest hell.

text.NRSV: The Lord tears down the house of the proud, but maintains the widow’s boundaries.
text.Rotherham: The house of proud men, will Yahweh tear down, but he will maintain the boundary of the widow.
text.KJV: The Lord will destroy the house of the proud: but he will establish the border of the widow.
text.NABRE: The Lord pulls down the house of the proud, but preserves intact the widow’s landmark.
text.DRB: Our Lord will destroy the house of the proud: and will make sure the borders of the widow.

text.NRSV: Evil plans are an abomination to the Lord, but gracious words are pure.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, are the plottings of the wicked, but, with the pure, are sayings of sweetness.
text.KJV: The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord: but the words of the pure are pleasant words.
text.NABRE: The schemes of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord, but gracious words are pure.
text.DRB: Evil cogitations are an abomination to our Lord: and pure speech most beautiful shall be confirmed of him.

text.NRSV: Those who are greedy for unjust gain make trouble for their households, but those who hate bribes will live.
text.Rotherham: He, troubleth his own house, who graspeth with greed, but, he that hateth gifts, shall live.
text.KJV: He that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house; but he that hateth gifts shall live.
text.NABRE: The greedy tear down their own house, but those who hate bribes will live.
text.DRB: He that pursueth avarice disturbeth his house: but he that hateth gifts shall live. By mercy and faith sins are purged: and by the fear of our Lord every one declineth from evil.

text.NRSV: The mind of the righteous ponders how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil.
text.Rotherham: The heart of the righteous, studieth to answer, but, the mouth of the lawless, belcheth out mischiefs.
text.KJV: The heart of the righteous studieth to answer: but the mouth of the wicked poureth out evil things.
text.NABRE: The heart of the just ponders a response, but the mouth of the wicked spews evil.
text.DRB: The mind of the just meditateth obedience: the mouth of the impious redoundeth with evils.

text.NRSV: The Lord is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: Far off is Yahweh, from the lawless, but, the prayer of the righteous, will he hear.
text.KJV: The Lord is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous.
text.NABRE: The Lord is far from the wicked, but hears the prayer of the just.
text.DRB: Our Lord is far from the impious: and he will hear the prayers of the just.

text.NRSV: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart, and good news refreshes the body.
text.Rotherham: The sparkling of bright eyes, rejoiceth the heart, Good news, giveth marrow to the bones.
text.KJV: The light of the eyes rejoiceth the heart: and a good report maketh the bones fat.
text.NABRE: A cheerful glance brings joy to the heart; good news invigorates the bones.
text.DRB: The light of the eyes rejoiceth the soul: a good name fatteth the bones.

text.NRSV: The ear that heeds wholesome admonition will lodge among the wise.
text.Rotherham: The ear that heareth the reproof which giveth life, in the midst of the wise, shall remain.
text.KJV: The ear that heareth the reproof of life abideth among the wise.
text.NABRE: The ear that listens to salutary reproof is at home among the wise.
text.DRB: The ear that heareth the reprehensions of life, shall abide in the midst of the wise.

text.NRSV: Those who ignore instruction despise themselves, but those who heed admonition gain understanding.
text.Rotherham: He that declineth correction, despiseth his own soul, but, he that heareth reproof, gaineth sense.
text.KJV: He that refuseth instruction despiseth his own soul: but he that heareth reproof getteth understanding.
text.NABRE: Those who disregard discipline hate themselves, but those who heed reproof acquire understanding.
text.DRB: He that rejecteth discipline, despiseth his soul: but he that yieldeth to reprehensions, is a possessor of the heart.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is instruction in wisdom, and humility goes before honor.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is the correction of wisdom, and, before honour, is humility.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.
text.NABRE: The fear of the Lord is training for wisdom, and humility goes before honors.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord, the discipline of wisdom: and humility goeth before glory.

text.NRSV: The plans of the mind belong to mortals, but the answer of the tongue is from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: To man, belong the preparations of the heart, but, from Yahweh, cometh the answer of the tongue.
text.KJV: The preparations of the heart in man, and the answer of the tongue, is from the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 Plans are made in human hearts, but from the Lord comes the tongue’s response.
text.DRB: It pertaineth to man to prepare the heart: and to our Lord to govern the tongue.

text.NRSV: All one’s ways may be pure in one’s own eyes, but the Lord weighs the spirit.
text.Rotherham: All the ways of a man, [may be] pure in his own eyes, but, he that testeth spirits, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but the Lord weigheth the spirits.
text.NABRE: All one’s ways are pure in one’s own eyes, but the measurer of motives is the Lord.
text.DRB: All the ways of man are open to his eyes: our Lord is the weigher of spirits.

text.NRSV: Commit your work to the Lord, and your plans will be established.
text.Rotherham: Roll, upon Yahweh, thy doings, that thy plans, may be established.
text.KJV: Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy thoughts shall be established.
text.NABRE: Entrust your works to the Lord, and your plans will succeed.
text.DRB: Reveal thy works to our Lord: and thy cogitations shall be directed.

text.NRSV: The Lord has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of trouble.
text.Rotherham: Everything, hath Yahweh made for its own purpose, yea, even the lawless one, for the day of calamity.
text.KJV: The Lord hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.
text.NABRE: The Lord has made everything for a purpose, even the wicked for the evil day.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath wrought all things for himself: the impious also to the evil day.

text.NRSV: All those who are arrogant are an abomination to the Lord; be assured, they will not go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, is every one who is haughty in heart, hand to hand, he shall not be held innocent.
text.KJV: Every one that is proud in heart is an abomination to the Lord: though hand join in hand, he shall not be unpunished.
text.NABRE: Every proud heart is an abomination to the Lord; be assured that none will go unpunished.
text.DRB: Every arrogant man is an abomination to our Lord: although hand shall be to hand he is not innocent. The beginning of a good way, is to do justice: and it is more acceptable with God, than to immolate hosts.

text.NRSV: By loyalty and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for, and by the fear of the Lord one avoids evil.
text.Rotherham: By lovingkindness and fidelity, shall iniquity be covered, and, in the revering of Yahweh, is a turning away from wrong.
text.KJV: By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the Lord men depart from evil.
text.NABRE: By steadfast loyalty guilt is expiated, and by the fear of the Lord evil is avoided.
text.DRB: By mercy and truth iniquity is redeemed: and in the fear of our Lord evil is avoided.

text.NRSV: When the ways of people please the Lord, he causes even their enemies to be at peace with them.
text.Rotherham: When, acceptable to Yahweh, are the ways of a man, even his enemies, doth he cause to make peace with him.
text.KJV: When a man’s ways please the Lord, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him.
text.NABRE: When the Lord is pleased with someone’s ways, he makes even enemies be at peace with them.
text.DRB: When the ways of man shall please our Lord, he will convert also his enemies to peace.

text.NRSV: Better is a little with righteousness than large income with injustice.
text.Rotherham: Better a little with righteousness, than large revenues, without justice.
text.KJV: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right.
text.NABRE: Better a little with justice, than a large income with injustice.
text.DRB: Better is a little with justice, than much fruit with iniquity.

text.NRSV: The human mind plans the way, but the Lord directs the steps.
text.Rotherham: A man’s heart, deviseth his way, but, Yahweh, directeth his steps.
text.KJV: A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.
text.NABRE: The human heart plans the way, but the Lord directs the steps.
text.DRB: The heart of man disposeth his way: but it pertaineth to our Lord to direct his progress.

text.NRSV: Inspired decisions are on the lips of a king; his mouth does not sin in judgment.
text.Rotherham: An oracle, is on the lips of a king, in giving sentence, his mouth must not be unfaithful.
text.KJV: A divine sentence is in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in judgment.
text.NABRE: An oracle is upon the king’s lips, no judgment of his mouth is false.
text.DRB: Divination is in the lips of the king, his mouth shall not err in judgment.

text.NRSV: Honest balances and scales are the Lord’s; all the weights in the bag are his work.
text.Rotherham: The balance and scales of justice, belong to Yahweh, and, his handiwork, are all the weights of the bag.
text.KJV: A just weight and balance are the Lord’s: all the weights of the bag are his work.
text.NABRE: Balance and scales belong to the Lord; every weight in the sack is his concern.
text.DRB: Weight and balance are judgments of our Lord: and his work all the stones of the bag.

text.NRSV: It is an abomination to kings to do evil, for the throne is established by righteousness.
text.Rotherham: The abomination of kings, is to work lawlessness, for, by righteousness, is established a throne.
text.KJV: It is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness: for the throne is established by righteousness.
text.NABRE: Wrongdoing is an abomination to kings, for by justice the throne endures.
text.DRB: They are abominable to the king that do impiously: because the throne is established by justice.

text.NRSV: Righteous lips are the delight of a king, and he loves those who speak what is right.
text.Rotherham: The delight of kings, are lips of righteousness,—and, the words of uprightness, he loveth.
text.KJV: Righteous lips are the delight of kings; and they love him that speaketh right.
text.NABRE: The king takes delight in honest lips, and whoever speaks what is right he loves.
text.DRB: The will of kings are just lips: he that speaketh right things shall be beloved.

text.NRSV: A king’s wrath is a messenger of death, and whoever is wise will appease it.
text.Rotherham: The wrath of a king, [meaneth] messengers of death, but, a man who is wise, will appease it.
text.KJV: The wrath of a king is as messengers of death: but a wise man will pacify it.
text.NABRE: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death, but a wise person can pacify it.
text.DRB: The king's indignation, messengers of death: and the wise man will pacify it.

text.NRSV: In the light of a king’s face there is life, and his favor is like the clouds that bring the spring rain.
text.Rotherham: In the light of a king’s countenance, is life, and, his good-pleasure, is like the cloud of harvest-rain.
text.KJV: In the light of the king’s countenance is life; and his favour is as a cloud of the latter rain.
text.NABRE: A king’s smile means life, and his favor is like a rain cloud in spring.
text.DRB: In the cheerfulness of the king's countenance is life: and his clemency is as the later shower.

text.NRSV: How much better to get wisdom than gold! To get insight is to be chosen rather than silver.
text.Rotherham: To acquire wisdom, how much better than gold! and, to get hold of understanding, more choice than silver!
text.KJV: How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver!
text.NABRE: How much better to get wisdom than gold! To get understanding is preferable to silver.
text.DRB: Possess wisdom, because it is better than gold: and get prudence, because it is more precious than silver.

text.NRSV: The highway of the upright avoids evil; those who guard their way preserve their lives.
text.Rotherham: The highway of the upright, is to avoid evil, He preserveth his soul, who guardeth his way.
text.KJV: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.
text.NABRE: The path of the upright leads away from misfortune; those who attend to their way guard their lives.
text.DRB: The path of the just avoideth evils: the keeper of his soul keepeth his way.

text.NRSV: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
text.Rotherham: Before grievous injury, pride! and, before a fall, haughtiness of spirit!
text.KJV: Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.
text.NABRE: Pride goes before disaster, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
text.DRB: Pride goeth before destruction, and before ruin the spirit shall be exalted.

text.NRSV: It is better to be of a lowly spirit among the poor than to divide the spoil with the proud.
text.Rotherham: Better is lowliness of spirit, with the patient, than a portion of spoil, with the proud.
text.KJV: Better it is to be of an humble spirit with the lowly, than to divide the spoil with the proud.
text.NABRE: It is better to be humble with the poor than to share plunder with the proud.
text.DRB: It is better to be humbled with the meek, than to divide spoils with the proud.

text.NRSV: Those who are attentive to a matter will prosper, and happy are those who trust in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that showeth discretion concerning a matter, shall find good, and, he that trusteth in Yahweh, how happy is he!
text.KJV: He that handleth a matter wisely shall find good: and whoso trusteth in the Lord, happy is he.
text.NABRE: Whoever ponders a matter will be successful; happy the one who trusts in the Lord!
text.DRB: The learned in word shall find good things: and he that hopeth in our Lord, is blessed.

text.NRSV: The wise of heart is called perceptive, and pleasant speech increases persuasiveness.
text.Rotherham: The wise in heart, shall be called intelligent, and, sweetness of lips, increaseth persuasiveness.
text.KJV: The wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.
text.NABRE: The wise of heart is esteemed for discernment, and pleasing speech gains a reputation for learning.
text.DRB: He that is wise in heart, shall be called prudent: and he that is sweet in speech shall find greater things.

text.NRSV: Wisdom is a fountain of life to one who has it, but folly is the punishment of fools.
text.Rotherham: A well-spring of life, is discretion to its owner, but, the correction of the foolish, is folly.
text.KJV: Understanding is a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools is folly.
text.NABRE: Good sense is a fountain of life to those who have it, but folly is the training of fools.
text.DRB: Afountain of life the learning of him that possesseth it: the doctrine of fools foolishness.

text.NRSV: The mind of the wise makes their speech judicious, and adds persuasiveness to their lips.
text.Rotherham: The heart of the wise, giveth discretion to his mouth, and, upon his lips, increaseth persuasiveness.
text.KJV: The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips.
text.NABRE: The heart of the wise makes for eloquent speech, and increases the learning on their lips.
text.DRB: The heart of the wise shall instruct his mouth: and shall add grace to his lips.

text.NRSV: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb, sweetness to the soul and health to the body.
text.Rotherham: A comb of honey, are pleasant words, sweet to the taste and healing to the bone.
text.KJV: Pleasant words are as an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.
text.NABRE: Pleasing words are a honeycomb, sweet to the taste and invigorating to the bones.
text.DRB: Well set words are a honey comb: sweetness of the soul the health of the bones.

text.NRSV: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way to death.
text.Rotherham: There is a way that enticeth a man, but, at the latter end thereof, are the ways of death.
text.KJV: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.
text.NABRE: Sometimes a way seems right, but the end of it leads to death!
text.DRB: There is a way that seemeth to a man right: and the later ends thereof lead to death.

text.NRSV: The appetite of workers works for them; their hunger urges them on.
text.Rotherham: The appetite of the toiler, hath toiled for him, for his mouth, hath urged him on.
text.KJV: He that laboureth laboureth for himself; for his mouth craveth it of him.
text.NABRE: The appetite of workers works for them, for their mouths urge them on.
text.DRB: The soul of him that laboureth doth labour to himself, because his mouth hath compelled him.

text.NRSV: Scoundrels concoct evil, and their speech is like a scorching fire.
text.Rotherham: An abandoned man, diggeth up mischief,—and, upon his lips, as it were a fire is scorching.
text.KJV: An ungodly man diggeth up evil: and in his lips there is as a burning fire.
text.NABRE: Scoundrels are a furnace of evil, and their lips are like a scorching fire.
text.DRB: The impious man diggeth evil, and in his lips fire burneth.

text.NRSV: A perverse person spreads strife, and a whisperer separates close friends.
text.Rotherham: A perverse man, sendeth forth strife, and, a tattler, separateth intimate friends.
text.KJV: A froward man soweth strife: and a whisperer separateth chief friends.
text.NABRE: Perverse speech sows discord, and talebearing separates bosom friends.
text.DRB: Aperverse man raiseth contentions: and one full of words separateth Princes.

text.NRSV: The violent entice their neighbors, and lead them in a way that is not good.
text.Rotherham: A ruthless man, enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him in a way, not good.
text.KJV: A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way that is not good.
text.NABRE: The violent deceive their neighbors, and lead them into a way that is not good.
text.DRB: An unjust man allureth his friend: and leadeth him by a way not good.

text.NRSV: One who winks the eyes plans perverse things; one who compresses the lips brings evil to pass.
text.Rotherham: Closing his eyes, to devise perverse things, biting his lips, he hath plotted mischief.
text.KJV: He shutteth his eyes to devise froward things: moving his lips he bringeth evil to pass.
text.NABRE: Whoever winks an eye plans perversity; whoever purses the lips does evil.
text.DRB: He that with astonished eyes thinketh wicked things, biting his lips bringeth evil to pass.

text.NRSV: Gray hair is a crown of glory; it is gained by a righteous life.
text.Rotherham: A crown of adorning, is a hoary head, in the way of righteousness, it should be found.
text.KJV: The hoary head is a crown of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousness.
text.NABRE: Gray hair is a crown of glory; it is gained by a life that is just.
text.DRB: Acrown of dignity old age, which shall be found in the ways of justice.

text.NRSV: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and one whose temper is controlled than one who captures a city.
text.Rotherham: Better he that is slow to anger, than a hero, and he that ruleth his spirit, than he that captureth a city.
text.KJV: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.
text.NABRE: The patient are better than warriors, and those who rule their temper, better than the conqueror of a city.
text.DRB: Better is the patient than a strong man: and he that ruleth his mind, than the overthrower of cities.

text.NRSV: The lot is cast into the lap, but the decision is the Lord’s alone.
text.Rotherham: Into the lap, is cast the lot, but, from Yahweh, is its every decision.
text.KJV: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Into the bag the lot is cast, but from the Lord comes every decision.
text.DRB: Lots are cast into the bosom, but they are ordered of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Better is a dry morsel with quiet than a house full of feasting with strife.
text.Rotherham: Better a dry morsel, and peace therewith, than a house full of contentious sacrifices.
text.KJV: Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 Better a dry crust with quiet than a house full of feasting with strife.
text.DRB: Better is a dry morsel with joy, than a house full of victims with brawling.

text.NRSV: A clever servant will rule over a son who acts shamefully, and will share the inheritance as one of the family.
text.Rotherham: A prudent servant, shall rule over a son who causeth shame, and, in the midst of brothers, shall he share the inheritance.
text.KJV: A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
text.NABRE: A wise servant will rule over an unworthy son, and will share the inheritance of the children.
text.DRB: Awise servant shall rule over foolish children, and divide inheritance among brethren.

text.NRSV: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace is for gold, but the Lord tests the heart.
text.Rotherham: Fining-pot for silver, crucible for gold, but, he that trieth hearts, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: The fining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold: but the Lord trieth the hearts.
text.NABRE: The crucible for silver, and the furnace for gold, but the tester of hearts is the Lord.
text.DRB: As silver is tried by fire, and gold in the furnace: so our Lord proveth the hearts.

text.NRSV: An evildoer listens to wicked lips; and a liar gives heed to a mischievous tongue.
text.Rotherham: Discord, giveth heed to the aggrieving lip,—Falsehood, giveth ear to the destroying tongue.
text.KJV: A wicked doer giveth heed to false lips; and a liar giveth ear to a naughty tongue.
text.NABRE: The evildoer gives heed to wicked lips, the liar, to a mischievous tongue.
text.DRB: The evil man obeyeth an unjust tongue: and the deceitful obeyeth lying lips.

text.NRSV: Those who mock the poor insult their Maker; those who are glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: He that mocketh the poor, hath reproached his Maker, He that maketh merry at distress, shall not be held innocent.
text.KJV: Whoso mocketh the poor reproacheth his Maker: and he that is glad at calamities shall not be unpunished.
text.NABRE: Whoever mocks the poor reviles their Maker; whoever rejoices in their misfortune will not go unpunished.
text.DRB: He that despiseth the poor, upbraideth his maker: and he that rejoiceth at another man's ruin, shall not be unpunished.

text.NRSV: Grandchildren are the crown of the aged, and the glory of children is their parents.
text.Rotherham: The crown of old men, consists of children’s children, and the adornment of children, is their fathers.
text.KJV: Children’s children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.
text.NABRE: Children’s children are the crown of the elderly, and the glory of children is their parentage.
text.DRB: The crown of old men the children's children: and the glory of children their fathers.

text.NRSV: Fine speech is not becoming to a fool; still less is false speech to a ruler.
text.Rotherham: Unseemly in an unworthy man, is the lip of excellence, much more, in one of noble mind, the lip of falsehood.
text.KJV: Excellent speech becometh not a fool: much less do lying lips a prince.
text.NABRE: Fine words ill fit a fool; how much more lying lips, a noble!
text.DRB: Eloquent words become not a fool, nor lying lips a Prince.

text.NRSV: A bribe is like a magic stone in the eyes of those who give it; wherever they turn they prosper.
text.Rotherham: A gift, in the eyes of its owner, is, a stone of beauty, whithersoever it turneth, it bringeth prosperity.
text.KJV: A gift is as a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoever it turneth, it prospereth.
text.NABRE: A bribe seems a charm to its user; at every turn it brings success.
text.DRB: Amost grateful pearl, the expectation of him that expecteth: whither soever he turneth himself, he understandeth wisely.

text.NRSV: One who forgives an affront fosters friendship, but one who dwells on disputes will alienate a friend.
text.Rotherham: He that hideth a transgression, seeketh love, but, he that repeateth a matter, separateth intimate friends.
text.KJV: He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends.
text.NABRE: Whoever overlooks an offense fosters friendship, but whoever gossips about it separates friends.
text.DRB: He that concealeth offence, seeketh friendships: he that in other word repeateth it, separateth the confederate.

text.NRSV: A rebuke goes deeper into one who has understanding than a hundred blows into a fool.
text.Rotherham: A reproof sinketh more deeply into an intelligent man than a hundred stripes, into a dullard!
text.KJV: A reproof entereth more into a wise man than an hundred stripes into a fool.
text.NABRE: A single reprimand does more for a discerning person than a hundred lashes for a fool.
text.DRB: Reprehension doth more profit with a wiseman, than an hundred stripes with a fool.

text.NRSV: Evil people seek only rebellion, and a cruel messenger will be sent against them.
text.Rotherham: Nothing less than rebellion, doth a wicked man seek, and, a relentless messenger, shall be sent out against him.
text.KJV: An evil man seeketh only rebellion: therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.
text.NABRE: The wicked pursue only rebellion, and a merciless messenger is sent against them.
text.DRB: An evil man always seeketh brawls: but a cruel angel shall be sent against him.

text.NRSV: Better to meet a she-bear robbed of its cubs than to confront a fool immersed in folly.
text.Rotherham: Let a bereaved bear encounter man, rather than a dullard, with his folly!
text.KJV: Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.
text.NABRE: Face a bear robbed of her cubs, but never fools in their folly!
text.DRB: It is better to meet a bear when her young are taken away, than a fool trusting to himself in his own folly.

text.NRSV: Evil will not depart from the house of one who returns evil for good.
text.Rotherham: He that returneth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
text.KJV: Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
text.NABRE: If you return evil for good, evil will not depart from your house.
text.DRB: He that rendereth evil things for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

text.NRSV: The beginning of strife is like letting out water; so stop before the quarrel breaks out.
text.Rotherham: A letting forth of water, is the beginning of strife, therefore, before it breaketh out, abandon, contention.
text.KJV: The beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water: therefore leave off contention, before it be meddled with.
text.NABRE: The start of strife is like the opening of a dam; check a quarrel before it bursts forth!
text.DRB: He that letteth water go, is the head of brawls: & before he suffer contumely, he forsaketh judgment.

text.NRSV: One who justifies the wicked and one who condemns the righteous are both alike an abomination to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that justifieth the lawless, and he that condemneth the righteous, an abomination to Yahweh, are they, both.
text.KJV: He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both are abomination to the Lord.
text.NABRE: Whoever acquits the wicked, whoever condemns the just— both are an abomination to the Lord.
text.DRB: He that justifieth the impious, and he that condemneth the just, both are abominable before God.

text.NRSV: Why should fools have a price in hand to buy wisdom, when they have no mind to learn?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a dullard? that he who is without sense, may acquire wisdom.
text.KJV: Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?
text.NABRE: Of what use is money in the hands of fools when they have no heart to acquire wisdom?
text.DRB: What doth it profit a fool to have riches, whereas he cannot buy wisdom? He that maketh his house high, seeketh ruin: and he that refuseth to learn, shall fall into evils.

text.NRSV: A friend loves at all times, and kinsfolk are born to share adversity.
text.Rotherham: At all times, doth a friend love, and, a brother for distress, must be born.
text.KJV: A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
text.NABRE: A friend is a friend at all times, and a brother is born for the time of adversity.
text.DRB: He loveth at all time that is a friend: and a brother is proved in distresses.

text.NRSV: It is senseless to give a pledge, to become surety for a neighbor.
text.Rotherham: A man lacking sense, is one who striketh hands, giving security, before his neighbour.
text.KJV: A man void of understanding striketh hands, and becometh surety in the presence of his friend.
text.NABRE: Those without sense give their hands in pledge, becoming surety for their neighbors.
text.DRB: Afoolish man will clap the hands, when he is surety for his friend.

text.NRSV: One who loves transgression loves strife; one who builds a high threshold invites broken bones.
text.Rotherham: A lover of transgression, is one who loveth strife, he that heighteneth his door, seeketh grievous harm.
text.KJV: He loveth transgression that loveth strife: and he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction.
text.NABRE: Those who love an offense love a fight; those who build their gate high court disaster.
text.DRB: He that meditateth discords, loveth brawls: and he that exalteth the door, seeketh ruin.

text.NRSV: A crooked mind does not prosper, and one with a perverse tongue falls into calamity.
text.Rotherham: The crooked in heart, shall not find good, and, he that is perverse with his tongue, shall fall into wickedness.
text.KJV: He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.
text.NABRE: The perverse in heart come to no good, and the double-tongued fall into trouble.
text.DRB: He that is of a perverse heart, shall not find good: and he that turneth his tongue, shall fall into evil.

text.NRSV: A fool born to anyone is a sorrow, and the father of a fool has no joy.
text.Rotherham: He that begetteth a dullard, it is to his own grief, neither can the father of the base, rejoice.
text.KJV: He that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow: and the father of a fool hath no joy.
text.NABRE: Whoever conceives a fool has grief; the father of a numskull has no joy.
text.DRB: Afool is born to his own ignominy: but neither shall the father rejoice in a fool.
